title: South Korea
dek: Eight days based in Seoul — palaces, markets, gaming districts and street food — with a weekend in Daejeon for MSI 2026. Everything below is checked; tick items off as you go and your progress saves.
footer: Location photos via Wikimedia Commons — tap any credit for its source, author and license.
intro: Seoul base, with a two-night trip to Daejeon for the MSI 2026 weekend (Jul 11–12). Seoul in July is hot and humid with monsoon rain — pack light layers and an umbrella.

sections.title: When you land
sections.body: You land at Terminal 1, Incheon Airport (~04:55 KST Thu Jul 9 — ⚠ confirm against your booking) on Air Premia YP132 from EWR Terminal B. The plan: clear immigration, shower at the airport to reset after the redeye, then take the Airport Limousine 6002 into Jongno. Budget 30–60 min through immigration at this hour. In order:
checklist: Have your e-Arrival Card QR ready on your phone — scan it at the automated immigration gate (download at e-arrivalcard.go.kr before you leave home; save it offline)
Pick up a T-money card at any convenience store or ticket machine in arrivals; load ≈₩20,000

Shower to reset: Spa on Air (rebranding to 'Spa at Home') on B1 by arrivals Door 3 — ≈₩10,000 for a 2-hour shower pass with towel + toiletries, open 24 h (⚠ confirm name/price on arrival). Keep a change of clothes in your carry-on so you're not opening checked bags for it

Buy an Airport Limousine 6002 ticket at the arrivals bus counter (card, ₩17,000 pp) and board at the T1 bus bay → get off at Gwanghwamun or Jongno 3-ga (~7-min walk to the base either side; ~75 min, first bus 05:35). Luggage rides under the bus — no stairs. Faster alternative: AREX All-Stop T1 → Seoul Station, then Line 1 one stop to Jonggak (~55 min total, ≈₩4,450) — Jonggak exits right by the base

From the bus stop, the Airbnb (Jongno — across from Olive Young Jonggak, ⚠ exact address to come) is a ~7-min walk or one Line-1 stop. After the redeye with bags, a short Kakao T taxi (≈₩4,000) is worth it
Text TRAVEL to 4004 to switch on Verizon TravelPass
Open KakaoMap or Naver Map — Google Maps navigation is very limited in Korea

sections.title: Local essentials
sections.body: Emergencies: 112 (police), 119 (fire & ambulance). Tap water is officially drinkable, though many locals prefer filtered or bottled. Tipping isn't expected. For maps use KakaoMap or Naver Map; for taxis, Kakao T; for translation, Papago. A little Korean goes far: annyeonghaseyo (hello), gamsahamnida (thank you).

sections.title: Entry & documents
sections.body: ⚠️ The entry rules below assume a US passport. On another passport, check your own requirements — they differ.
checklist: No visa for tourism up to 90 days
K-ETA is waived for US citizens through Dec 31, 2026 — you don't need it
Without a K-ETA you must complete the free e-Arrival Card within 3 days before arrival at the official site (e-arrivalcard.go.kr) — ignore lookalike sites that charge a fee
Optional: you may still apply for a K-ETA (₩10,000, ~$8, valid 3 years) to skip the arrival card
Passport valid for the length of your stay
Carry your MSI ticket confirmations and a power adapter (Korea uses Type C/F, 220V)

sections.title: Booking checklist
sections.body: Roughly in this order — the first group is what sells out or pins your dates.

Air Premia baggage (economy, EWR route): free 2 × 23 kg checked + 1 carry-on ≤10 kg (≤115 cm total) — but Economy Lite is 15 kg only, so ⚠ confirm which fare you booked before you pack; check the current YP132 carry-on notice and keep power banks in your carry-on (Air Premia rule)

MSI tickets — loaded in the NOL World app (mobile-only; no screenshots, prints, or transfers) ✓

KTX for all three — Seoul→Daejeon (Jul 11 morning) plus the Monday Busan day-trip legs Daejeon→Busan (≈10:30) and Busan→Seoul (≈18:30 evening), 3 tickets each leg — book all three early; Finals weekend + summer-weekend trains fill fast — letskorail.com or Korail Talk app
Changdeokgung Secret Garden — book the 10:30 English Huwon tour for Tue Jul 14 (50 online slots, sells out)
e-Arrival Card — within 72 hours of your flight
Confirm Verizon TravelPass; download KakaoMap, Naver Map, Papago, Kakao T, Korail
Break in your shoes / insoles (week before)

sections.title: Pre-flight countdown — specific actions by date
sections.body:
Time-sensitive. Do these in order — the e-Arrival Card window opens exactly 72 h before landing; do not complete it earlier or it expires.

Now — before Jul 7
Book the KTX Seoul→Daejeon (Jul 11, morning departure ≈08:30–09:00 — 3 tickets; all three going to Daejeon) and the Monday Jul 13 Busan day-trip legs — Daejeon→Busan (≈10:30) and Busan→Seoul (≈18:30 evening; full plan in the Monday day entry) — atkorail.com or the KorailTalk app — Finals weekend trains fill fast. Your Daejeon I-Hotel (27-5 Expo-ro 123beon-gil, Yuseong-gu) is booked for both nights (Jul 11 & 12) — it sits ~130 m / a 2-minute walk from DCC II, so you drop your bags and stroll to both matches. Book the Changdeokgung Secret Garden (Huwon) 10:30 English tour for Tue Jul 14 — official info via cdg.go.kr (50 online slots per session; sells out). Install the NOL World app and confirm MSI tickets open (mobile-only — no screenshots, no prints).

Jul 5 (Sun) — 3 days before
Start the jet lag clock: go to bed 1 hour earlier than normal tonight, wake 1 hour earlier. Get 15–20 min of bright outdoor light within 30 min of waking. Dim screens after 20:00. Take 0.5–1 mg melatonin ~5 h before your new (earlier) bedtime. Stock Pokémon GO: Max Revives, Ultra Balls, Golden Razz, Silver Pinaps, Charged TMs. Build and save raid teams for Mega Mewtwo X (Flying/Fairy) and Y (Ghost/Dark).

Jul 6 (Mon) — 2 days before
Jet lag: go to bed 2 hours earlier. Same morning light + evening dim + melatonin protocol. Clear Pokémon storage to 400+ (transfer/release before the trip).

Jul 6–7 — within 3 days of landing
Complete the e-Arrival Card at e-arrivalcard.go.kr (free, 5 min). The site accepts submissions from ~3 days (72 h) before arrival — for your ~04:55 KST Jul 9 landing that window opens around Jul 6; don't try earlier or it won't be accepted, and don't leave it to the airport. Save the QR code to your camera roll offline. Jet lag: bedtime 3 hours earlier if possible. No caffeine after 12:00. Charge all battery packs and GO Plus/Gotcha. Pack: flip-flops (for jimjilbang), compact umbrella, quick-dry layers, insoles.

Jul 8 (Wed) — departure night
Depart Newark Liberty Airport (EWR) — not JFK on Air Premia YP132 from Terminal B (dep ≈01:00 EDT — ⚠ confirm against your booking; Air Premia has changed this schedule before). K-ETA is waived for US citizens through Dec 31 2026 (no action needed). Confirm your Air Premia bag allowance and pre-pay any extra/overweight bags online — far cheaper than at the counter; power banks ride in your carry-on, never checked. Check-in opens ~6 h before departure (≈19:00 Jul 7 EDT). Arrive at EWR by 22:30 at the latest for a smooth international process.
checklist: KTX Jul 11 Seoul → Daejeon (morning, ≈08:30–09:00) booked — 3 tickets — letskorail.com or KorailTalk
KTX Jul 13 Busan day trip booked: Daejeon → Busan (≈10:30) and Busan → Seoul (≈18:30 evening)
Daejeon I-Hotel (27-5 Expo-ro 123beon-gil) — 2 nights (Jul 11 & 12) booked; 2-min walk to DCC II
Changdeokgung Secret Garden tour Jul 14 reserved
e-Arrival Card completed at e-arrivalcard.go.kr — QR saved offline
MSI tickets loaded and tested in NOL World app (no screenshots)

Departure airport confirmed: Newark Liberty EWR — not JFK

sections.title: Jet lag protocol — EWR to Seoul (13-hour eastward shift)
sections.body:
A 13-hour eastward shift is near worst-case for jet lag. Natural adaptation: ~1 hour per day = 13 days to fully adjust. With this protocol: 4–5 days. Three tools: timed light exposure, melatonin, and sleep scheduling. Sources: Frontiers in Physiology (2019), PMC1249488/PMC1262683, CDC Yellow Book Travel Health, Huberman Lab.

Pre-flight (Jul 5–7): Shift bedtime 1 hour earlier each night (see Pre-flight countdown above). Each morning get bright outdoor light within 15–30 min of waking — this is the strongest phase-advance signal available. Dim screens after 20:00 each evening (blocks the delay signals fighting your advance). Take 0.5–1 mg melatonin approximately 5 hours before your shifted bedtime — timing is critical; at this point melatonin produces maximum clock advance. Taking it too late works against you.

On the flight (01:30+ EDT Jul 8): Sleep is the goal. Eye mask and earplugs on immediately. Keep window shade closed — the polar route crosses near-continuous daylight at altitude. No alcohol: it suppresses REM and fragments sleep. No caffeine from boarding onward. Drink ~250 ml water per hour (cabin humidity is under 20%). Take 0.5–1 mg melatonin shortly after takeoff (~02:00 EDT / ~15:00 KST). Your body still reads this as ~2 a.m., so the priority on this leg is simply to sleep the overnight flight — melatonin helps you do that. (The real Seoul re-anchoring happens with morning light after you land.) When breakfast service begins near landing, eat it: meal timing helps shift your metabolic clock to Seoul rhythms.

Day 1 in Seoul (Jul 9 landing): Your internal clock says ~17:00 EDT (early evening New York). Do not nap on arrival — a full sleep cycle now locks you to NY time for days. Get outside within 1 hour of reaching your room: even 15–20 min of outdoor morning light (07:00–10:00 KST) is your strongest circadian anchor. Caffeine: use it in the morning and early afternoon only — hard cutoff at 14:00 KST. Stay active; don't sit still. Avoid bright outdoor light 17:00–19:00 KST for the first 2 days — on your NY body clock this is just-after-sunrise, and light here delays rather than advances your clock. Jimjilbang visit 17:00–21:00 (see Day 1 itinerary): the heat-then-cooling cycle triggers the temperature drop that signals sleep onset — one of the best jet-lag recovery tools available. Target bedtime: 22:00–22:30 KST. Take 0.5–1 mg melatonin at 21:00 KST. Repeat nightly for 4–5 nights.
checklist: Jul 5: Bedtime 1h earlier, morning outdoor light within 30 min of waking, dim evenings, melatonin 5h before new bedtime
Jul 6: Bedtime 2h earlier — same protocol
Jul 7: Bedtime 3h earlier; no caffeine after 12:00
On flight: eye mask on, no alcohol, water every hour, melatonin at ~02:00 EDT, eat Seoul-time breakfast near landing
Jul 9 AM: Outside for light by 09:00 KST — no nap over 30 min, caffeine cut off at 14:00 KST
Jul 9 PM: Jimjilbang 17:00–21:00 KST, melatonin 0.5–1 mg at 21:00, bed by 22:30
Jul 10–13: Morning outdoor light daily; avoid light 17:00–19:00 KST first 2 days; melatonin 21:00 nightly

sections.title: Phone & data
sections.body:
Verizon TravelPass — text TRAVEL to 4004 to activate ($12/line/day). Easy and reliable. A local eSIM is roughly half the price if your phone is unlocked:

  • Korean eSIMs from KT, SKT, or LG U+ run about ₩26,000–35,000 (~$17–23) for 7–10 days of unlimited LTE data. Speed throttles to 5 Mbps after 3 GB/day but stays usable for maps and translation. Buy in advance at KT Roaming, Trazy, or KKday — or at a telecom counter in ICN T1 arrivals (open from ≈07:00 KST on arrival morning). Confirm your phone is carrier-unlocked before you leave home.
  • Resellers ByteSIM and TooSIM often have the same networks at slightly lower prices with English checkout.

Apps that actually matter: KakaoMap or Naver Map for directions (Google Maps routing is severely limited in Korea), Kakao T for taxis, Papago for translation — download the Korean offline language pack before you leave (Papago → Languages → Korean → Download), Korail / KorailTalk for KTX tickets.

sections.title: Weather & comfort
sections.body:
Early-to-mid July is Korea's monsoon season (jangma) — hot (30–35 °C / 86–95 °F), very humid, and rainy. Gear up accordingly:

  • Pack a compact umbrella — heavy rain can hit without warning and briefly flood low streets or cause metro delays. Check the morning forecast.
  • Moisture-wicking, quick-dry clothes — linen or synthetic shirts, not cotton. Cotton stays wet all day in high humidity. One change of shirt per day minimum.
  • Hydrate constantly — convenience stores have cold water and sports drinks every 200m; use them. Heat exhaustion is a real risk by day 3 if you're walking all day.
  • Build in AC breaks every 2 hours: a café, a mall (Lotte Young Plaza, Shinsegae, Coex), or a museum. These are free and essential, not optional.
  • Weather app: use WeatherKorea or the KMA official app (kma.go.kr) — Korean meteorological forecasts are more accurate than international apps for localised monsoon patterns.

sections.title: Holidays & your dates
sections.body:
Timing is in your favour: no public holidays fall during 8–15 July 2026. Constitution Day (17 July) isn't a day off and lands just after you leave, and the next real holiday is Liberation Day on 15 August — so nothing shuts down while you're there.

What you do need to plan around is the weather. Mid-July is the peak of Korea's monsoon, 'jangma' — the wettest, most humid stretch of the year, with roughly 300–400 mm of rain across about 16 days, real heat, and humidity near 80%. The rain often arrives in heavy bursts that can briefly flood streets or slow transit, with a small typhoon chance later in the month. Pack a compact umbrella and quick-dry layers, keep indoor options in your back pocket, and check the forecast each morning. Your MSI weekend in Daejeon (11–12 July) is indoors, so rain won't touch it. (See Weather & comfort for what to wear.)

sections.title: Rainy day fallback — when the monsoon hits
sections.body:
Expect at least 1–2 full-rain days in July. The rain can be heavy but rarely lasts the whole day. Have a plan ready:

  • Stay underground: Seoul's subway-connected underground malls are enormous. The Hongdae Shopping Street (under Hongik University Station) and Gangnam's COEX Underground Mall are both walkable and free to enter — good for an entire afternoon.
  • COEX Aquarium (Samseong Station, Line 2): 45,000+ sea creatures, 2–3 hours easy. ≈₩33,000–35,000 pp — ⚠ verify price and book online to skip the queue. Open daily.
  • Seoul Museum of Art (SeMA) (City Hall, Line 1/2): free admission for main exhibitions, strong contemporary Korean art, fully dry. Takes 1.5–2 hours.
  • PC Bang: Full-spec gaming PCs, ≈₩1,500–2,500/hr, unlimited snacks at the counter. There's one within 5 minutes of any Hongdae station exit. Unironically one of the best rainy-day options for this group.
  • Cinema: CGV or Megabox near Hapjeong Station (one stop from Hongdae). Korean cinemas are excellent — huge screens, reclining seats, ≈₩12,000–15,000 pp. Both apps have English filter for subtitled showings.
  • Rainy day = great raiding: Fewer players competing for gyms. Head to COEX, Times Square (Yeongdeungpo, Line 1/5), or Lotte World Tower (Jamsil, Line 2/8) — all have indoor gyms and PokéStops. Light rain also boosts Water-type Incense spawns.

sections.title: Final checks — before you fly
sections.body: Two items are still open; the rest are one-time verifications before packing.
checklist: ⚠ Book KTX: Seoul → Daejeon (Jul 11 morning, ≈08:30), then the Jul 13 Busan day-trip legs — Daejeon → Busan (≈10:30) and Busan → Seoul (≈18:30 evening). Finals + summer weekend fills fast — book now at letskorail.com or in the Korail Talk app. Seoul–Daejeon standard ≈₩23,700; the Daejeon→Busan and Busan→Seoul fares run higher — ⚠ check current fares on Korail. Main Seoul Station, not Suseo/SRT.
⚠ Book Changdeokgung Secret Garden (Huwon) tour. English 10:30 slot on Jul 14. Book at cdg.go.kr — sells out weeks ahead. Palace ticket (₩3,000) + Huwon tour (₩5,000) = ₩8,000/person total.
T-money card topped up to ≈₩50,000 per person before leaving NJ, OR load at ICN T1 arrivals (GS25 / convenience store, no fee).
Kakao T app installed on all three phones — use for Daejeon taxis (Jul 11) and Daejeon + Busan taxis (Jul 13).
Pokémon GO: bag cleared to ≥500 free slots, Remote Raid Passes stocked, Lucky Eggs and Star Pieces packed, charged TMs in bag for GO Fest.
NOL World app installed on both Daejeon-trip phones; MSI tickets confirmed showing in the app (name-tied — no screenshots, prints, or transfers).
e-Arrival Card (electronic): complete at e-arrivalcard.go.kr within 72 hours of landing — easiest to fill out on the plane.
Re-verify GO Fest Global boss list the week of Jul 8. Niantic adjusts lineups up to 48 hours before an event; the counters in this guide are for the announced roster.
Cash: ≈₩200,000–300,000 per person (convenience stores + street food are often cash-only). Alternatively, load Wise or Revolut before departure for fee-free ATM withdrawals.
Power adapter: Korea uses 220V Type C/F round-pin plugs (same as continental Europe). One adapter per person — USB-C PDs are fine.
Heat prep packed: hat (arena AC won't help you outdoors), sunscreen SPF 50+, electrolyte tablets. Seoul in July averages 28–31°C with high humidity.
Leave home contact a copy of your itinerary: Seoul Jul 8–10 & Jul 13–15 (Busan day trip Jul 13), Daejeon Jul 11–12 (Daejeon I-Hotel, 27-5 Expo-ro 123beon-gil, Yuseong-gu).

sections.title: Public holidays
sections.note: Checked automatically against your travel dates (source: Nager.Date, refreshed each deploy). On a Korean national holiday, expect many shops, banks and government offices to close or run reduced hours, and major sights and transit to be busier than usual.

sections.title: Money & currency
sections.body:
Korea uses the won (₩). The won is weak right now, which helps your spending power — roughly (approximate, check before you go): ₩1,000 ≈ $0.67 and ₩10,000 ≈ $6.70, at about ₩1,535 to the dollar.

Cards and contactless work almost everywhere. For cash, the most reliable option is an ATM inside a GS25 or CU convenience store — both accept most foreign cards and charge modest fees. Global ATM Alliance banks (KEB Hana is the Korean partner) have lower or zero foreign withdrawal fees if your home bank is in the network — check before you leave. Avoid airport exchange booths; the rates are poor. Keep ≈₩30,000–50,000 cash for markets and small eateries. A T-money card covers all subway and buses. Tipping isn't expected anywhere.

sections.title: Health & pharmacy
sections.body:
Emergencies: dial 119 for ambulance or fire (free) and 112 for police. On a 119 call, say 'English, please' to reach an interpreter — your location is auto-detected. Save 1330 first: the 24/7 Korea Travel Hotline, English-speaking, can interpret, give directions, and patch you through to 119 or 112. 1339 is a 24-hour medical line that locates the nearest ER.

US Embassy Seoul — for lost passports, serious medical emergencies, or legal trouble: 188 Sejong-daero, Jongno-gu (between Gyeongbokgung and City Hall). Emergency line: +82-2-397-4114 (24/7). Website: kr.usembassy.gov. If you lose your passport in Daejeon, go to the Embassy in Seoul — there's no consulate in Daejeon.

Pharmacies (약국, yakguk) show a '약' sign. Many close Sundays but major-station branches stay open; some run 24 hours. Convenience stores cover paracetamol and antacids after hours. Korea bills upfront — no insurance card means 100% out of pocket.
checklist: Save 119 (ambulance/fire), 112 (police), 1330 (24/7 English travel hotline), 1339 (24-hr medical ER finder)
US Embassy Seoul — 188 Sejong-daero, Jongno-gu · +82-2-397-4114 (24/7) — for lost passports or serious emergencies
Buy travel insurance — without it you pay 100% upfront
Pack medicines in original packaging, plus a note of their generic names
Learn the sign: 약 = pharmacy; convenience stores cover basics after hours

sections.title: Etiquette & language
sections.body:
Korean is the language; signs, the subway, and major sights are bilingual. For translation, use Papago — it's far more accurate than Google Translate for menus and signs. Google Maps barely works for directions in Korea — use KakaoMap or Naver Map.

  • Bowing: a slight nod works as hello, thank you, or goodbye — don't overthink it.
  • Two-handed exchange: give and receive cards, cash, or items with both hands (or right hand with the left touching your forearm) — it signals respect.
  • No tipping, anywhere. Genuinely none — attempting it can create awkwardness. The price on the menu is what you pay.
  • Chopstick etiquette: don't stand them upright in rice (funeral association); rest them on the holder when you're not using them.
  • Pouring drinks: pour for others at the table; let them pour for you. Don't self-pour at a shared meal.
  • Shoes off: watch for a raised floor at traditional floor-seating restaurants — it's obvious when you see it.
  • Volume: keep it quiet indoors, especially on transit.
  • Subway rules: no eating or drinking on the metro — even water bottles, technically. Pink seats at the end of every car are priority seats for elderly, disabled, and pregnant passengers — don't sit in them even if the car is empty. You'll notice even young Koreans avoid them. Phones on silent; keep conversations brief and quiet.
  • Papago offline: before you leave home, open Papago → Languages → Korean → download the offline pack. Camera translation still needs a data signal, but text input works offline — useful in rural Daejeon areas.

Key phrases (Hangul + romanisation):

  • 안녕하세요 — annyeonghaseyo — hello
  • 감사합니다 — gamsahamnida — thank you (formal)
  • 얼마예요? — eolmayeyo? — how much?
  • 이거 주세요 — igeo juseyo — this one, please
  • 화장실 어디예요? — hwajangsil eodiyeyo? — where is the bathroom?
  • 괜찮아요 — gwaenchanayo — it's okay / no problem

sections.title: Navigating Seoul as first-time foreigners
sections.body:
Seoul is safe and welcoming to international tourists — most of your interactions will be frictionless. A few things worth knowing:

  • Language hesitation ≠ hostility. If someone seems reluctant, it's almost always communication anxiety. Open Papago's camera mode, point it at the menu or sign — translation overlays live. Showing a typed phrase on screen resolves almost anything. Most staff near tourist areas have functional English.
  • Tattoos. Some traditional jimjilbangs, public pools, and older gyms still ban visible tattoos. SPAREX Dongdaemun: check their current policy on the Klook listing before Day 1 if this applies to any of you. Restaurants and the other venues in this guide are unbothered.
  • Nightlife. Hongdae and Sinchon clubs have diverse, ID-at-the-door entry. High-end Gangnam clubs have documented discriminatory door policies — not worth the uncertainty. Hongdae is your nightlife district and it's excellent. Noraebangs (private karaoke rooms) and PC bangs are universally welcoming.
  • Restaurants. Almost no restaurant refuses foreign visitors. Even local spots manage — worst case, picture menu and point. See the Food section for ordering mechanics.
  • If you're turned away anywhere: don't escalate. Seoul is dense enough that an equivalent alternative is within two minutes. Move on.

sections.title: What to wear — July in Korea
sections.body:
July is 30–35 °C and brutally humid. What you pack will make or break day 3 when the heat exhaustion hits.

  • Shirts: linen or moisture-wicking synthetic only. Cotton saturates and stays wet all day in 80% humidity — you'll feel it by noon. Pack one quick-dry shirt per day minimum; they rinse and dry overnight in the hotel.
  • Bottoms: lightweight shorts or thin chinos — both are fine in Seoul. Shorts are totally normal in the city; Korean dress culture is casual outside of formal workplaces. Avoid jeans in July: they trap heat and dry slowly.
  • Shoes: comfortable walking shoes with broken-in soles. You'll walk 10–15 km/day on mixed surfaces (stone palace courtyards, uneven sidewalks, tiled subway stations). Sandals are fine for Hongdae evenings but not a full sightseeing day. If any of you haven't broken in your shoes yet — do it this week.
  • Compact folding umbrella — non-negotiable. Heavy rain hits without warning during jangma. A good compact umbrella folds to 25 cm and costs under $15 at home; convenience stores sell them but they're flimsy.
  • Indoor layer (thin jacket or cardigan). Korean malls, restaurants, subway trains, and arenas run AC at 16–18 °C. The temperature swing between outside (34 °C) and inside (18 °C) is the most common reason people get sick mid-trip. Keep a light layer in your bag at all times — critical for DCC II during MSI.
  • MSI / arena: team jerseys and esports gear are welcome; no dress code at DCC II beyond the no-huge-bag rule. Bring that AC layer.
  • Jimjilbang (Day 1, SPAREX): the facility provides shorts and a T-shirt — you need nothing special except flip-flops for walking between the pools. Leave your shoes at the locker.
  • Daejeon day: same as Seoul. You'll be outdoors at EXPO Science Park for 6+ hours before the match — quick-dry fabric matters most on Saturday.

sections.title: Language guide — speaking Korean
sections.body:
English is widely spoken in central Seoul and tourist areas; much less common in Daejeon outside DCC II. You don't need to be fluent — three tools handle almost everything: Papago (camera translation; download Korean offline pack before you leave), picture menus (point and hold up fingers), and a handful of phrases below. Koreans respond warmly to any attempt, however imperfect.

Pronunciation note: stress is fairly even across syllables. Vowels are consistent: a = 'ah', eo = 'uh', eu = lips-back 'uh', i = 'ee', u = 'oo'.

Greetings & basics

  • 안녕하세요 — annyeonghaseyo — hello (universal; a slight nod with this is perfect)
  • 감사합니다 — gamsahamnida — thank you (formal)
  • 괜찮아요 — gwaenchanayo — it's okay / I'm fine / no problem
  • 네 — ne — yes  ·  아니요 — aniyo — no
  • 죄송합니다 — joesonghamnida — I'm sorry / excuse me (bumping into someone)
  • 잠깐만요 — jamkkanmanyo — just a moment / one second

Restaurants & cafes

  • 여기요! — yeogi-yo! — over here! (the universal way to call a server — normal and expected)
  • 이거 주세요 — igeo juseyo — this one, please (point at the menu)
  • 두 개 주세요 — du gae juseyo — two of these, please (hold up fingers to show number)
  • 맵지 않게 해주세요 — maepji ank-e haejuseyo — not spicy, please (show this on Papago if unsure of pronunciation)
  • 물 주세요 — mul juseyo — water, please
  • 계산해 주세요 — gyesan haejuseyo — the bill, please (or just walk to the counter)
  • 얼마예요? — eolmayeyo? — how much is this?
  • 카드 돼요? — kadeu dwaeyo? — do you accept card?

Shopping

  • 봐도 돼요? — bwado dwaeyo? — may I look / try this?
  • 이거 사이즈 있어요? — igeo saijeu isseoyo? — do you have this in my size?
  • 더 싸게 해주세요 — deo ssage haejuseyo — can you give a discount? (markets only)
  • 영수증 주세요 — yeongsujeung juseyo — receipt, please (keep for VAT refund)

Transit & directions

  • 화장실 어디예요? — hwajangsil eodiyeyo? — where is the bathroom?
  • 여기로 가 주세요 — yeogiro ga juseyo — please take me here (show address on phone to driver)
  • 미터기로 가주세요 — miteo-giro gajuseyo — please use the meter (taxis)
  • 세워 주세요 — seweo juseyo — please stop here
  • 영어 하세요? — yeong-eo haseyo? — do you speak English?

Emergencies

  • 도와주세요! — dowajuseyo! — help me!
  • 경찰 불러주세요 — gyeongchal bulleojuseyo — please call the police (112)
  • 구급차 불러주세요 — gugeupcha bulleojuseyo — please call an ambulance (119)
  • 아파요 — apayo — I'm sick / it hurts
  • 병원 어디예요? — byeongwon eodiyeyo? — where is the hospital?

sections.title: What staff will say to you
sections.body:
When you walk into a restaurant, shop, or hotel, you'll hear these immediately. Knowing them removes the guesswork.

In restaurants:

  • 어서 오세요! — eoseo oseyo! — welcome! (said by every server; a nod is fine, no response needed)
  • 몇 분이세요? — myeot buniseyo? — how many people? (hold up fingers)
  • 주문하시겠어요? — jumunhashigeseoyo? — are you ready to order? (wave gently + say jamkkanmanyo if not)
  • 기다려 주세요 — gidaryeo juseyo — please wait a moment
  • 카드요, 현금이요? — kadeunyo, hyeongeumiyo? — card or cash? (point to your card or pocket)
  • 영수증 드릴까요? — yeongsujeung deurilkkayo? — would you like a receipt? Say ne (yes) — keep it for VAT refund
  • 맛있게 드세요! — masikke deuseyo! — enjoy your meal! (nod is fine)

In shops:

  • 어떤 거 찾으세요? — eotteon geo chaeuseyo? — what are you looking for? (open Papago or point)
  • 품절이에요 — pumjeoliheyo — out of stock / sold out
  • 세금 환급 되세요? — segeum hwangeup doeseyo? — would you like a tax refund? Say ne and show your passport to get the VAT refund in-store.

sections.title: Key transit routes
steps: Incheon → Jongno base — Airport Limousine Bus 6002 (your pick): Air Premia arrives at Terminal 1. Buy a 6002 ticket at the arrivals bus counter (card, ₩17,000 pp) and board at the T1 bus bay — it runs T1 → Hongik Univ. → Sinchon → Ewha → Gwanghwamun → Jongno 3-ga. Get off at Gwanghwamun or Jongno 3-ga — each a ~7-min walk from the base. ~75 min (first bus 05:35, last 23:30). Luggage stows under the bus — no stairs, no transfer. Faster alternative: AREX to Seoul Station (All-Stop ≈45 min ≈₩4,050, or Express ≈₩11,000), then Line 1 one stop to Jonggak — right by the base, but you carry bags through the subway.

Around Seoul from the Jongno base (Jonggak Station, Line 1): subway signed in English — tap T-money in and out; use Kakao T for taxis. Key hops from Jonggak: Gyeongbokgung & the palaces → 12–15 min walk north; Insadong / Bukchon → 5–12 min walk; LoL Park → right at Jonggak Exit 1; Seoul Station (KTX) → Line 1 south 2 stops (~5 min, ≈₩1,400); Gwangjang Market → Line 1 east 2 stops to Jongno 5-ga (~4 min); Myeongdong / Olive Young → 12-min walk or Line 1 to Euljiro (~10 min); Hongdae → Line 2 via Euljiro, or AREX from Seoul Station (~25 min); SPAREX Dongdaemun jimjilbang → Line 1 east to Dongdaemun (~8 min).

Seoul → Daejeon (Jul 11, all 3 of you): KTX from Seoul Station (not Suseo/SRT), ~50 min — book on the Korail app or korail.com. Take a morning train departing ≈08:30–09:00 KST to arrive Daejeon by ≈09:20–09:50 — you'll be raiding GO Fest at the EXPO Science Park by 10:00. Two of you go into the arena for the LBF at 17:00; the third stays in Daejeon, raids and hangs near the EXPO area.

Daejeon Station → your hotel / DCC II: a taxi is the easy call — ~20 min, ≈₩11,000 (≈₩3,700 each, split three ways). Your Daejeon I-Hotel (27-5 Expo-ro 123beon-gil) is ~130 m — a 2-minute walk — from the arena, so drop bags and walk in. Cheaper backup: Metro Line 1 to Government Complex Daejeon (정부청사역, ~15 min) then a short taxi (<10 min, ≈₩6,500). Skip the direct bus 707 — it takes over an hour. DCC II: 87 Expo-ro, Yuseong-gu.

Monday Jul 13 — Busan day trip, then back to Seoul: You stay a second night in Daejeon (no Sunday-night train). Monday you ride Daejeon → Busan (KTX ≈10:30, ~1 hr 25 min), spend the afternoon in Busan, then Busan → Seoul (KTX ≈18:30, ~2 hr 30 min) arriving Seoul ≈21:00–21:30. Book both legs in advance — summer-weekend trains fill fast. Full plan in the Monday day entry. (Too wiped after MSI? Skip Busan and take a morning KTX Daejeon → Seoul instead — see the Monday contingencies.)

Jongno → Incheon (Jul 15) — Airport Limousine Bus 6002: Air Premia YP131 departs ICN Terminal 1 at 21:30. Board the 6002 at the Gwanghwamun or Jongno 3-ga stop (₩17,000 pp, ~75 min; allow up to 2 hr if you leave in the 17:00–20:00 rush). To keep a 3+ hour airport buffer, board by ~14:30–15:00 so you reach ICN by ~16:00–17:00 — unhurried check-in, tax refund, and duty-free. Alternative: AREX from Seoul Station (Line 1 two stops from Jonggak) — fixed time, dodges road traffic, the safer choice if you leave later. Luggage delivery: to travel hands-free on the final morning, a same-day airport luggage courier can collect bags from the Airbnb and deliver them to the ICN departures counter — book a day ahead; ⚠ confirm the operator, cutoff time, and price before relying on it.

sections.title: Getting to/from Incheon Airport — options compared
sections.body:
Your Jongno base sits on subway Line 1 (Jonggak) and near two 6002 limousine-bus stops (Gwanghwamun, Jongno 3-ga) — central and well-connected to the airport. Your plan is the Limousine Bus 6002 both ways — comfortable coach seats, luggage stowed under the bus, no subway stairs. Fares/times per Airport Limousine Co. (⚠ verify before travel).

  • Limousine Bus 6002 (your pick) — ICN T1 ⇄ Gwanghwamun / Jongno 3-ga: ≈75 min, ₩17,000/person (first bus from ICN 05:35, last 23:30). Coach seats, under-bus luggage; a ~7-min walk from the base. Buy at the bus counter in T1 arrivals (card). ⚠ In the 17:00–20:00 rush it can take up to 2 hr — on departure day, board by ~14:30–15:00 to keep a 3+ hr airport buffer.
  • AREX (subway train) — ICN T1 → Seoul Station, then Line 1 one stop to Jonggak: ≈50–55 min total, ≈₩4,050 (All-Stop) or ₩11,000 (Express). No traffic risk — the safer choice if you leave later than ~15:00. Jonggak exits right by the base; you carry bags through the subway.
  • Taxi (Kakao T) — ICN ⇄ the base: ≈45–55 min, ≈₩55,000–65,000 total (≈₩18,000–22,000/person split 3). Fastest door-to-door, no bag handling; Incheon Bridge toll ≈₩2,000 included. Book 'Large Taxi' in the Kakao T app for 3 people + luggage.
  • Luggage delivery — a same-day airport luggage courier can move bags Airbnb ⇄ ICN so you travel hands-free on arrival or departure day. Book a day ahead; ⚠ confirm operator, cutoff, and price.

Bottom line: Limousine Bus 6002 both ways — board early on the Jul 15 departure to beat the evening rush, or take AREX via Seoul Station if you leave after ~15:00.

sections.title: Tips & tricks — Seoul and Daejeon
sections.body:
Maps: Use Naver Map or KakaoMap — both show exact exit numbers (e.g. Exit 5) which are essential in Seoul's deep multi-exit stations. Google Maps cannot legally access Korean road data at full resolution — it works for landmarks but gives actively wrong transit routing. Delete it from your muscle memory for this trip.

Subway: tap T-money in and out. If you forget to tap out, the system charges you the maximum fare — tap out every time. Announcements are in Korean, English, Chinese, and Japanese. The colored line number and station name on the platform are always in English. When in doubt, count stops.

Taxis: Kakao T shows the estimated fare and driver details before you get in — always use it at night or in unfamiliar areas. Flagging a taxi is fine in the city. In Daejeon, English is much less common; show the driver the destination in Korean on your phone (type the address in Naver Map and hand them the phone). Kakao T works exactly the same in Daejeon.

Paying for food: in most Korean restaurants you pay at the counter when you leave, not at the table. There's no waiting for a check. Some places have a QR code at the table to scan and pay — it still goes to the counter. Kiosks at chain restaurants are touchscreen-only; tap the English flag at the top-right corner.

Call button: every sit-down restaurant table has a call button to summon a server. Press it when you're ready to order — don't wave or make eye contact trying to flag someone down. Some are physical buttons, some are embedded in a screen.

Convenience stores as infrastructure: GS25 and CU are your ATMs, rest stops, phone chargers (some have USB outlets), umbrellas, and late-night kitchens. You will visit them multiple times per day. The hot-food section (fried chicken pieces, tteokbokki cups, steamed buns) is genuinely good and cheap.

Power and charging: Korea uses Type C/F plugs, 220V. Most modern USB-C chargers handle 110–240V automatically — check your brick. iPhones need a plug adapter; no voltage converter required. Many cafés and PC bangs have power outlets at every seat.

Luggage: if you check out but have hours left in Seoul, most convenience stores and Seoul Station's left-luggage counter (level B1, Platform area) store bags by the hour. Your Jongno host may also allow a late bag drop.

Daejeon specifically: it's a smaller city. English signage exists around DCC II and the EXPO district but disappears quickly. Use Naver Map even for short walks — the street layout around Yuseong-gu is less grid-like than central Seoul. The Dunsan-dong entertainment district (~15 min taxi from I-Hotel) is where the restaurants and bars are on Sunday evening.

items.body:
A night flight across the Pacific — the trip starts in the dark and ends with a Korean sunrise.

  • Depart EWR ≈01:00 EDT (Terminal B). Air Premia YP132 — ≈14–15 hrs, land ICN Terminal 1 ≈04:55 KST Thu Jul 9 (⚠ confirm times against your booking).
  • On the plane: Eye mask + earplugs immediately. No alcohol. Water every hour (~250 ml). Melatonin 0.5–1 mg at ~02:00 EDT. Window shade closed — polar route has near-continuous daylight. Eat the breakfast service near landing.
  • On landing: e-Arrival Card QR at immigration → T-money card at arrivals → AREX All-Stop to Hongik University Station (≈49 min, ≈₩4,050 — no transfer). See 'When you land' checklist for full steps.

items.note: The most important day of the trip for jet lag. Follow the protocol exactly — especially the bedtime.
items.body:
After 15 hours in the air, Seoul delivers exactly what exhausted travellers need: a city that works perfectly at 6 a.m.

  • ~05:30 — shower at ICN. Clear immigration, grab a T-money card, then reset at Spa on Air (B1 by arrivals Door 3, ≈₩10,000/2 hr, towel + toiletries; ⚠ confirm name/price). A hot shower after the redeye is the difference between a usable day and a write-off.
  • ~06:30 — Airport Limousine Bus 6002 → Jongno. Buy at the T1 arrivals bus counter (card, ₩17,000 pp) and board 6002; get off at Gwanghwamun or Jongno 3-ga (~75 min at dawn, no traffic; first bus 05:35). Luggage rides under the bus. From the stop, a ~7-min walk or short Kakao T to the base — Jongno, across from Olive Young Jonggak (⚠ exact address to come). Drop bags — do not sleep.
  • ~09:00 — outdoor light. Step out along Cheonggyecheon for 20 min of morning light — the single strongest clock anchor. Spin the dense Jongno PokéStops if you want (Road of Legends live — skip raiding today, save passes for tomorrow).
  • 10:00–17:00 — stay awake. You're steps from Insadong, Cheonggyecheon, and Gwangjang Market — an easy flat loop with an AC café break every 90 min. Goal: awake, upright, in daylight. Don't commit to real sightseeing.
  • 17:00–21:00 — SPAREX Dongdaemun jimjilbang. Line 1 to Jongno 3-ga, then Line 5 two stops to Dongdaemun H&C Park (~12 min) → Exit 14, B3F Goodmorning City (247 Jangchungdan-ro, Jung-gu). Entry ₩12,000 if you enter by 20:00 (≈₩7,000 late night after 20:00 — ⚠ verify on Klook), pay on-site; open 24 h. Heated rooms + cooling breaks. ⚠ Verify tattoo policy on the Klook listing if relevant.
  • ~21:00 — dinner + bed. Walk-in Korean BBQ near the base — samgyeopsal joints line Jongno and Insadong 5 min away (see Where to eat). Melatonin 0.5–1 mg at 21:00. In bed by 22:30 — the most important sleep of the trip.

items.body:
Grand palaces and stone lanes a few minutes from your door, the home of the LCK one stop away, and a Hongdae raid night to close it out.

  • Getting there: a 12–15 min walk north, or Line 1 to Jongno 3-ga → Line 3 to Gyeongbokgung Station (~10 min). Taxi: ~10 min, ≈₩6,000.
  • Morning — Gyeongbokgung. Guard ceremony ≈10:00 and 14:00 (⚠ verify current schedule). Free entry with hanbok rental at gate. Last entry 17:30.
  • Midday — Bukchon Hanok Village. Uphill from the palace — real residents, keep voices low. Spin PokéStops as you wander.
  • Early afternoon — Insadong. Tea houses and craft galleries, ~5 min walk from Bukchon. Iced coffee stop before the next stop.
  • ~15:30 — LoL Park (롤파크). Walk ~10 min south from Insadong to Jonggak (Line 1) Exit 1 — LoL Park is in the Gran Seoul Building, 33 Jong-ro, right by your base. Open 10:00–19:00. Exhibits trace LCK history from Season 1; Riot store kiosk and LCK shop carry MSI tie-in merch you'll want before Daejeon. Cafe Bilgewater for a drink. Budget 45–60 min. Free entry to exhibits.
  • ~17:00 — head toward Hongdae or any gym-dense area. Tonight is the Road of Legends finale — one of the best raiding evenings of the trip.
  • 18:00–19:00 — Raid Hour: Origin Forme Dialga & Palkia. Best Master League forms (Roar of Time / Spacial Rend), shiny-eligible (1/20), 2–4 trainers. 4–5 passes. See Road of Legends counter charts.
  • 19:00–20:00 — Primal Raid Hour: Primal Kyogre & Groudon. Groudon = quadruple Water weakness, Master League XL Candy, best Xurkitree counter Sunday. 5+ trainers needed — coordinate ahead. 3–4 passes.

Road of Legends ends 23:59 KST. Dinner in Hongdae after 20:00 — dozens of walk-in options right there. Before you subway back to Jongno: T1 Basecamp (마포구 양화로 147, B1) is right by the Hongdae gyms. Half gaming museum, half esports cafe — sit at pro-level setups, order a themed drink, browse the merch shop (open until 22:00). Perfect cap to the night before you head to Daejeon tomorrow.

items.note: Leaving in the morning instead of afternoon gives you the full 9-hour GO Fest window in Daejeon — all three habitats, no transit cutoff, and you're already 130 m from the arena when the LBF starts.
items.body:
All three head south — GO Fest in the morning, two of you into the arena at 17:00.

  • 08:00 — leave the base. Line 1 from Jonggak, 2 stops to Seoul Station (~5 min, ≈₩1,400) — the central base puts you one short hop from the KTX. Or a quick taxi (~10 min, ≈₩6,000).
  • ~08:30 — KTX to Daejeon. Board the ≈08:30–09:00 KST service south. Main Seoul Station only — not Suseo/SRT. Platform varies; check the Korail app day-of. 50-min ride, arrive Daejeon ≈09:20–09:50.
  • Daejeon Station → I-Hotel. Kakao T taxi (~20 min, ≈₩11,000 — split three ways ≈₩3,700 each). Drop bags at the desk (room available from check-in time; they will hold luggage either way). EXPO Science Park is a 2-min walk east — active gyms and PokéStops across the plaza.

🎮 10:00–16:30 — GO Fest Global Day 1, Daejeon. You have all three Saturday habitats without a transit cutoff:

  • Stormfire Peaks 10:00–13:00 — Ice/Electric/Fire: Lugia, Ho-Oh, Articuno, Zapdos, Moltres, Raikou, Entei, Suicune (5★, all shiny); Mega Blaziken, Mega Ampharos, Mega Abomasnow.
  • Astral Tides 13:00–16:00 — Psychic/Ghost/Water: Dialga, Palkia, Giratina (Altered & Origin), Xerneas, Yveltal, lake trio, Solgaleo, Lunala (5★); Mega Alakazam, Mega Gengar, Mega Swampert.
  • Dragonflight Summit 16:00–19:00 — Flying/Rock/Dragon: Kyogre, Groudon, Rayquaza, Reshiram, Zekrom, Kyurem (5★, all shiny); Mega Salamence, Mega Pidgeot, Mega Aerodactyl. Raid from EXPO Science Park 16:00–16:30, then walk to the arena.

Top priority: Mega Mewtwo X (Super Mega, Saturday-only, global debut) — appears in every habitat. Spend 3–5 of your 9 passes here; fill the rest on Stormfire (Lugia/Ho-Oh XL Candy) and Dragonflight (Rayquaza, Groudon, Kyogre).

  • 16:30 — wrap up, walk to DCC II. 130 m from the hotel. Merch stand before security — Day 1 queue is shorter before 16:45. Bag limit: over 45.7 × 57 × 19 cm = back to hotel first.
  • 17:00 KST — MSI Lower Bracket Final. Passport on you — tickets are name-tied, mobile-only via NOL World app (no screenshots, prints, or transfers). Best-of-5, budget 3–5 hrs. The arena runs aggressive AC — bring a layer.

After the LBF (can run to ~22:00): Cham-i-mat (gamjatang, last order 21:30) or Gobang Sikdang (grill, book ahead) if you wrap by ~21:00; otherwise see Post-match dinner in Where to eat for the Dunsan-dong late-night strip. I-Hotel room is a 2-min walk.

🎮 Third person during the LBF (17:00–22:00): Keep raiding the Dragonflight Summit habitat (16:00–19:00) from the EXPO Science Park plazas — you have the full remaining GO Fest window with no arena cutoff. Hanbat Arboretum (~1.5 km away) is a good backup if you want a change of scenery and shade. Meet the others at the hotel after the match. Dinner together — same post-match options above.

items.body:
The day the season ends — and with a second Daejeon night, you get to enjoy all of it.

🎮 GO Fest Global Day 2 — Daejeon (10:00–19:00 KST): You're in the Expo/Yuseong district all day. Raid from your hotel area and on the walk to breakfast. EXPO Science Park plazas are the densest PokéStop/gym cluster; Hanbat Arboretum (~1.5 km away) is a backup if you want a change of scenery.

  • Earthforged Domain 10:00–13:00 — Ground/Steel/Normal: Regirock, Regice, Registeel, Regieleki, Regidrago, Regigigas, Heatran, Origin Dialga, Origin Palkia, all Therian/Incarnate formes of the Forces of Nature (5★, all shiny). Mega Metagross, Mega Garchomp, Mega Audino.
  • Verdant Anomaly 13:00–16:00 — Poison/Bug/Grass: Deoxys (all 4 formes), Genesect (all 5 drives), Necrozma, Stakataka, the Tapu quartet, Ultra Beasts. Korea (Asia-Pacific) regional: Xurkitree — only catchable on this continent. Raid it before 15:00. Mega Beedrill, Mega Pinsir, Mega Sceptile.
  • Twilight Battlefield 16:00–19:00 — Dark/Fairy/Fighting: Latias, Latios, Cresselia, Darkrai, Cobalion, Terrakion, Virizion, Zacian, Zamazenta (5★, all shiny). Mega Tyranitar, Mega Gardevoir, Mega Lucario. Overlaps the Grand Final — raid on arena breaks, finish the window after the match (you're staying the night).

Top priority:

  • Mega Mewtwo Y (Super Mega, Sunday-only, global debut, shiny) — every habitat, 3–5 passes
  • Xurkitree (Verdant 13–16, Korea-only UB) — must raid before the 15:00 GF, 2–3 passes
  • Secondary: Therian Landorus/Thundurus + Heatran (Earthforged); Latios + Darkrai (Twilight); Necrozma (Verdant, fusion fodder)

🏟 MSI Grand Final, 15:00 KST. Head over by 14:15 — it's a 2-min walk from the I-Hotel. Best-of-5, likely ends 18:00–20:30. NOL World mobile ticket; passport on you.

🎮 Third person during the Grand Final (15:00–≈20:30): Twilight Battlefield habitat runs 16:00–19:00 — finish it from the EXPO Science Park plazas while the other two are inside. Darkrai, Latios, Zacian, Zamazenta; Mega Tyranitar and Mega Lucario. Best use of the overlap window short of having a third ticket.

Celebrate in Daejeon — all three, no train to catch. Gobang Sikdang Expo Branch (고반식당, 엑스포로151번길 19, 2F — ~5–10 min walk from I-Hotel; book ahead on 042-863-2104, open Sat–Sun from 12:00) is the premium grill pick. Or head to Dunsan-dong (~15–20 min taxi) for Daejeon's full nightlife strip.

items.note: The hard-charging weekend is done. Take the Daejeon morning slow — Sungsimdang first, a short walk, lunch, then head to Busan for the afternoon reward. If anyone is running on empty after two GO Fest days and MSI, see the contingency at the bottom and ride straight to Seoul instead.
items.body:
Daejeon delivered. Now spend an afternoon in a city that smells like the sea.

08:00–09:00 — Sungsimdang DCC. Walk from I-Hotel (~2 min) to Sungsimdang DCC Branch (107 Expo-ro, Yuseong-gu). Two things: fried soboro (튀김소보로 — sweet crumble bun, deep-fried) and chive bread (부추빵). Queue moves fast. Eat standing outside or on an EXPO bench. Open 08:00.

09:00–09:30 — EXPO / Gapcheon walk. Short decompression loop through the Expo Science Park grounds (15–20 min), then back to check out of I-Hotel. Wide outdoor plazas and waterfront benches — a slow exhale after two hard days. No checklist, just walk.

~09:30 — Ossi Kalguksu Doryong (오씨칼국수 도룡점). 도룡동 4-13, Yuseong-gu, ~5 min walk from I-Hotel. ⚠ Arrive before 11:00 — fills fast once lunch starts. The Doryong branch is the genuine article. Surf-clam broth (동죽조개탕): anchovies + surf clams, mildly spiced, clean. ≈₩9,000–12,000 pp. Walk-in only. Naver Map.

~10:30 — Kakao T to Daejeon Station. ≈20 min, ≈₩11,000 split. Daejeon Station only — not Daejeon West. Board the KTX south toward Busan (check Korail app for the 10:30–11:15 departure window). ≈1 hr 25 min to Busan Station. Book the return leg in advance (≈18:30 departure from Busan).

🐟 Busan: Jagalchi + Gukje Market — ≈12:00–13:30.

Kakao T from Busan Station to Jagalchi Market (~15 min, ≈₩9,000–11,000). No itinerary — the market tells you what to do.

  • Each person's mission: one fish cake (어묵), one street snack (hotteok, tteokbokki, or a shellfish cup from an auntie stall). Show her what you want; she handles it. Cash preferred (₩50,000 accepted; small bills easier).
  • Jagalchi Market (남구 자갈치해안로 52, Nam-gu) — indoor building + surrounding outdoor stalls. Open 05:00–22:00 (⚠ closed first and third Tuesday — you're Monday, no issue). Naver Map.
  • Gukje Market (중구 신창동 4가) — 5-min walk from Jagalchi; clothes, household goods, and covered food alleys. No must-buy, but worth a wander. Naver Map.

🌊 Gwangalli Beach — ≈14:00–17:00.

Taxi from Jagalchi to Gwangalli (~25 min, ≈₩12,000–15,000). Drop at Gwanganhaebyeon-ro (광안해변로) — you're at the beach.

  • Walk the shoreline. The Gwangan Bridge (광안대교) fills the horizon. Best for photos at dusk but the wide arc works in any flat July light.
  • Buy iced coffee or watermelon juice from a beach vendor. Sit on the low wall or sand. This is the reward for two days of organized intensity.
  • Optional: Millak Waterside Park (밀락더마켓) is a short walk west — modern food court + sea-facing terrace. Good for a drink break.

🍜 Dinner — Gaon Milmyeon (가온밀면) — ≈17:00.

109 Minrak Subeon-ro (2F), Suyeong-gu, Busan — a 5-min walk or short taxi from Gwangalli Beach. Open 10:00–21:00, last order 20:30. Gwangan Bridge visible from the 2F window. Busan milmyeon (밀면): wheat noodles, lighter than buckwheat naengmyeon, served cold with beef broth or spicy sauce. Locals' favourite; no tourist markup. ≈₩9,000–12,000 pp ⚠ — verify price on-site. Naver Map.

~17:30 — taxi to Busan Station for the ≈18:30 KTX back to Seoul.

Busan Suyeong-gu → Busan Station: ≈30–35 min by taxi, ≈₩18,000–22,000. Busan Station only — not Gupo or Singyeongju. KTX north: ≈2 hr 30 min → Seoul Station ≈21:00–21:30. From Seoul Station: Line 1 → Jonggak (2 stops, ~5 min, ≈₩1,400). Or taxi ~10 min, ≈₩6,000. Home by ~22:00.

⚠ Contingencies

  • If you two are wiped after MSI: skip Busan entirely. Morning KTX direct Daejeon → Seoul (~50 min). Afternoon at Gwangjang Market or DDP, or just rest at the base. The city is still there tomorrow.
  • If running behind schedule: skip the Gapcheon walk + Ossi Kalguksu; taxi straight to Daejeon Station after Sungsimdang. Keep Jagalchi + Gwangalli; drop Gukje Market.
  • If it rains in Busan: swap Gwangalli for Shinsegae Spa Land — Shinsegae Centum City, Centum-daero, Haeundae-gu. Korea's largest department spa; ≈₩22,000 entry; 22 themed sauna rooms, rooftop pool. Busan Metro Line 2 from Busan Station to Centum City Station. Open 06:00–24:00. Naver Map.

items.note: Book the 10:30 English Huwon tour ahead (50 online slots per session — it sells out). This is your last proper night, and the flight home isn't until 21:30 tomorrow, so linger over the last dinner and a final wander.
items.body:
The palace garden you saved for last, then the day's shopping in one clean sweep — central gifts first, Stüssy across the river, and a garden-galbi send-off.

  • Morning — Changdeokgung & Secret Garden. From the base: a ~12-min walk to Anguk, or Line 1 to Jongno 3-ga → Line 3 one stop to Anguk Station. Arrive 30 min early for the pre-booked 10:30 English Huwon tour (70–90 min guided walk, gentle terrain). Note: the palace itself requires a separate ₩3,000 admission ticket at the gate; the Huwon tour (₩5,000) is on top of that.
  • ~12:30 — central gifts first (K-beauty + K-pop, the for-others run). Down into Insadong from Anguk for a quick souvenir sweep (Ssamziegil, hanji, tea, ceramics), then Myeongdong (Line 3→4, or ~15-min walk): Olive Young Myeongdong Town — 2 floors, and crucially it does the tax refund at the register + stores your bags, so the K-beauty gifts ride out with the VAT already knocked off and never weigh you down; then Line Friends Square and the Myeongdong K-pop street for albums, photocards and BT21/NewJeans merch. Keep every receipt. Save the bulk K-beauty for the departure-morning run near the base — today stays light. (Optional: Kyobo Book Centre Gwanghwamun for stationery.)
  • ~16:00 — Apgujeong: Stüssy + streetwear. Line 3 to Apgujeong (~20 min). Stüssy Seoul Chapter (42 Apgujeong-ro 46-gil, Gangnam; 11:00–20:00) for the merch run, Gentle Monster nearby (art-installation eyewear, free to walk through), a coffee at Kasina Cafe upstairs. This is the one river crossing — and you end the night right here.
  • ~18:30 — Samwon Garden (삼원가든) — the send-off dinner.
    Where: 835 Eonju-ro, Gangnam — ~7-min walk from Apgujeong Station (Line 3, Exit 3), just past Stüssy. Iconic since 1976: premium Hanwoo galbi grilled over charcoal in a serene Korean garden. Michelin Guide–listed, warm and relaxed (no dress code), built for a group.
    Price: ≈₩40,000–50,000+ pp for Hanwoo ⚠ confirm on Catch Table.
    When it fits: right after Stüssy — no backtracking; you're already in Apgujeong. Budget ~2 hours.
    Book: Catch Table or +82-2-548-3030 (open Tue, dinner from 17:00). Book before you leave home.
  • Prefer to end central? → A Flower Blossom on the Rice (꽃,밥에피다). 3-6 Insadong 16-gil, Jongno-gu — Michelin Green Star organic Korean; the signature Bojagi Bibimbap (five sautéed vegetables in a thin egg omelet, tied like a gift). Calm, unhurried, no dress code. ≈₩120,000 pp ⚠ verify on Catch Table. If the group would rather finish in the old-Seoul lanes by the base, do Stüssy a touch earlier and loop Line 3 back to Anguk (~20 min) for an 18:30 seating here. Book: Catch Table Global / +82-2-732-0276. Book before you leave home.

If both are booked out — other Tuesday options (advance reservation via Catch Table):

  • Mishmash (미쉬매쉬) — near Changdeokgung Palace; hanok-chic setting, traditional fermentation-forward plating. ≈₩130,000 pp. Walking distance from the morning's Secret Garden — the most logistically tight option if you stay in the Anguk area all day. Reserve via Catch Table.
  • Soseoul Hannam, a common Hannam pick, is closed Mon–Tue — out for a Tuesday send-off.
  • Bloom in the Palace Seolleung — Gangnam; 14F city-view dining, court-cuisine concept incorporating Korean beef and heritage dishes. ≈₩100,000+ pp. CatchTable page. More accessible on short notice than the others — worth checking for same-week slots.

After dinner — your last night, no early alarm. Ending at Samwon Garden you're in Apgujeong/Gangnam — stroll the Rodeo streets and Cheongdam, or take a short hop to a Han River park for a low-key riverside close; head back to the base whenever (~30 min, Line 3). If you looped back central for Flower Blossom instead, Ikseon-dong's hanok lanes and Insadong are right at your door. Either way the flight home isn't until 21:30 tomorrow — stay out as late as you like.

items.note: Yesterday was the big shopping day, so today stays easy — a slow Jongno morning, luggage handled early, and an unhurried airport run that lands you at ICN with a 3+ hour cushion before the 21:30 flight. No cross-town dashes, no hard stops.
items.body:
Coffee, one last wander, then a comfortable ride to the airport.

  • ~09:30 — check out the Jongno base. Bags packed last night. Two ways to handle luggage: (a) hand them to a same-day airport luggage courier in the morning (Airbnb → ICN departures) so you wander hands-free — book a day ahead, ⚠ confirm operator + cutoff + price; or (b) keep them — the 6002 stows luggage under the bus, so it's easy either way.
  • Late morning — a slow Jongno loop (all walkable from the base, no transit):
    • Olive Young Jonggak — right across the street: the big K-beauty gift haul, saved for last so it goes straight into your bags. Passport out for the in-store tax refund; then hand the load to the luggage courier or check it at ICN — no skincare bag hauled through the heat.
    • Insadong — final souvenir sweep (hanji, tea, ceramics) and Ssamziegil, 5–10 min on foot.
    • Ikseon-dong hanok lanes — one last café or brunch a few minutes east.
    • Snack-souvenir haul at a mart or GS25/CU (honey-butter chips, teas, ramyeon), or save it for ICN duty-free.
  • ~14:30 — Airport Limousine Bus 6002 from Gwanghwamun / Jongno 3-ga → ICN Terminal 1. ₩17,000 pp, buy at the stop kiosk (card). ~75 min; leaving now beats the worst of the evening rush and lands you at ICN ~16:00–17:00 — 4+ hours before the 21:30 flight, exactly the cushion you want. Full stop location + timing in Key transit routes.
  • At ICN T1: Air Premia check-in and bag drop, then the tax-refund kiosk in departures (all stamped receipts), and a last Olive Young / skincare duty-free pass — often cheaper than street price. Plenty of time to eat airside and decompress. YP131 departs T1 at 21:30.

Optional — gaming detour (only if you skipped it Tuesday): if Gunpla, figures, and trading cards at Yongsan i'Park Mall (Animate i'Park inside) are a must, ride Line 1 south to Yongsan in the morning, then back to the base for the bus. It reintroduces cross-town movement — better folded into Tuesday if you can, but the option is here.
