sections.title: Solo in Seoul — a menu for whenever someone splits off
sections.body:
You're all in Daejeon for GO Fest weekend, but if anyone peels off from the group at any point — a rest day, a different pace, someone skipping an activity — this is a menu, not a schedule. Wake up, see how you feel, pick one or two things. Seoul rewards aimless wandering more than almost any city, and it's one of the safest places in the world to explore alone. Everything starts from the Jongno base (Jonggak, Line 1); check exact hours on Naver Map that morning, and keep an indoor option in your pocket (July is monsoon).

To recharge quietly

  • Ikseon-dong & Cheonggyecheon — hanok café lanes and a landscaped stream, both minutes from the base. Café-hop, read by the water, people-watch. The lowest-effort good day in Seoul.
  • Starfield Library, COEX (Samseong, Line 2, ~40 min) — a giant free indoor library atrium; cool, calm, quietly impressive. Pair with the mall + aquarium if it rains. Naver Map.
  • Kyobo Book Centre (Gwanghwamun) or any Hongdae book café — big English sections; hours vanish happily.
  • A second jjimjilbang if Day 1's SPAREX landed well — pure decompression, no conversation required.

For nature and a view (great alone)

  • Inwangsan — a beginner-friendly granite peak (338 m, ~1.5–2 hr round trip) lined with the old fortress wall and skyline views. Start at Gyeongbokgung Station (Line 3) Exit 1, ~10-min walk to the trailhead (~15–20 min from the base). Locals hike it and the wall is lit at night, so it feels safe solo — the clean antidote to GO Fest crowds. Naver Map.
  • Naksan Park + Ihwa Mural Village (Hyehwa, Line 4) — an easy hilltop walk along the city wall; low effort, big payoff near dusk.
  • A Han River park (Ttukseom or Yeouido) — sit by the water with convenience-store ramen, rent a mat for a few thousand won. The most quietly Seoul thing you can do alone.

For art & culture (indoors — heat/rain-proof)

  • National Museum of Korea (Ichon — ~25 min via Seoul Station) — vast and world-class, free permanent collection. Open Sat to 21:00, Sun to 18:00; half a day easy. Naver Map.
  • MMCA Seoul (Samcheong) — contemporary art, central, ≈₩2,000–4,000, open weekends. Leeum (Hannam) — striking modern + traditional collections, ≈₩5,000–10,000, closed Mon and usually needs an online reservation a few days ahead.
  • Deoksugung (City Hall) — a smaller, central palace to take at your own pace; the group's two palace days are different ones, so this stays fresh.

If you're curious about Korea's game culture (no need to be a gamer)

  • LoL Park (Jonggak, Line 1) — the Riot store and exhibits are walk-in; even as a non-player it's a striking slice of Korea's esports world (see Shopping).
  • Hongdae arcades and claw machines, a PC bang for an hour, or Yongsan i'Park Mall (electronics, figures, cards) and Animate (Hongik Exit 4) — all casual and easy solo.

To eat well alone — Korea is mostly solo-friendly, with a couple of exceptions:

  • Easy alone: noodle shops (kalguksu, naengmyeon), gimbap, a counter seat at Gwangjang Market, mall food courts, convenience-store feasts by the river.
  • Harder alone: Korean BBQ and most grills expect two or more — save those for when the group's back.
  • A dessert / café crawl in Yeonnam-dong or the hanok alleys of Ikseon-dong (Jongno-3-ga) is a great low-key afternoon.

Evening, your pace — Hongdae buskers and street food are right outside the door; a film at CGV or Megabox Hapjeong (look for English-subtitled showings); or the Han River at night. If you want company with zero planning, Mike's Cabin and Dokkaebi are walk-in and foreigner-friendly (see Hongdae after dark).

Text the group during the match, and reunite in Seoul when they're back Monday. And if some morning the honest answer is 'sleep in, ramen, then wander Hongdae' — that's a perfect Seoul day too. No pressure to fill it.

items.name: Gyeongbokgung
items.body: The largest of the Joseon palaces, with the changing-of-the-guard ceremony and the banquet hall over a lotus pond. Summer hours run to about 18:30 (last entry 17:30); closed Tuesdays, and free if you're wearing a hanbok.

img.file: Gyeonghoeru (Royal Banquet Hall) at Gyeongbokgung Palace, Seoul.jpg

items.name: Bukchon Hanok Village
items.body: Lanes of restored hanok houses on the hillside between the palaces, framed against the modern skyline. It's a living neighbourhood — keep noise down and don't peer into homes.

img.file: Bukchon-ro 11-gil street with hanok houses and blue sky in Bukchon Hanok Village Seoul.jpg

items.name: N Seoul Tower (Namsan)
items.body: On the wooded peak of Namsan, with the best wraparound view of the city. Ride the cable car or a bus up rather than hiking in the heat; the observation deck is worth timing for sunset.

items.name: Dongdaemun Design Plaza
items.body: Zaha Hadid's vast silver, curving 'spaceship', best after dark when it's lit and the LED-rose field is on. Design shops, exhibitions and the surrounding night markets make it an easy evening.

img.file: Dongdaemun Design Plaza at night, Seoul, Korea.jpg

items.name: Cheonggyecheon
items.body: A restored stream cutting through the centre with walkways below street level — a cool, easy stroll and a good flat break between sights.

img.alt: Cheonggyecheon stream and stepped weir at Cheonggye Plaza, Seoul

items.name: Insadong
items.body: A walkable lane of galleries, traditional craft shops and tea houses, a short hop from the palaces — a natural pairing with a Gyeongbokgung morning.

img.alt: Traditional restaurant alley in Insadong, Seoul

sections.title: Visiting tips
sections.body: Time the palaces around their closed days — Gyeongbokgung is shut Tuesdays, Changdeokgung Mondays — and pre-book the Changdeokgung Secret Garden, which only enters on timed guided walks. Bukchon is residential, so it's best early and quiet. And don't fight the July heat: treat the air-conditioned stops (DDP, COEX, museums) as the spine of the hottest afternoons.

sections.title: Your MSI weekend
sections.body:
The Mid-Season Invitational runs at the Daejeon Convention Center II (Hall 2), the first international League event ever held in Daejeon. Your tickets cover the Lower Bracket Final on Sat Jul 11 and the Grand Final on Sun Jul 12. Your Daejeon I-Hotel is ~130 m from the hall — a 2-minute walk — both days you stroll over.

What to bring:

  • Photo ID (passport) — tickets are name-tied and scanned at entry via the NOL World app
  • Bag no larger than 45.7 cm × 57 cm × 19 cm (~18" × 22" × 7.5"), must fit under your seat. DCC II has no bag check — oversized bags are turned away at the door with nowhere to leave them. The I-Hotel is 2 min away, so drop anything large before you walk over.
  • Outside food is allowed if sealed or in an appropriate container — saves money vs. arena concessions
  • Portable battery pack — phone needs to last all day for the NOL World ticket scan, live coverage, and GO Fest raids before/after

Prohibited (per the official Riot event policy): glass containers, cans, laser pens, air horns, banners or flags on poles, drones, selfie-sticks, laptops and tablets. Non-compliant items may be confiscated without return.

The merch queue is longest before the first match on Day 1 — arrive 60–90 min early if you want MSI merchandise. Day 2 line is shorter.

sections.title: Getting to the arena
sections.body:
From Seoul Station, the KTX reaches Daejeon in about 50 minutes (book on the Korail app or korail.com; use Seoul Station, not Suseo/SRT). From Daejeon Station, a taxi to the arena is ~20 min, ≈₩11,000 (split three ways = ≈₩3,700 each); the cheaper metro-plus-taxi combo runs ~25 min via Government Complex Daejeon (정부청사역). The arena is Daejeon Convention Center II (DCC II), 87 Expo-ro, Yuseong-gu — and your Daejeon I-Hotel (27-5 Expo-ro 123beon-gil) is ~130 m from it, a 2-minute walk, so you stroll to both matches and skip the post-match scramble entirely.

Monday Jul 13 — you don't ride straight back: You are staying both Saturday and Sunday nights at the I-Hotel, so there is no rush after the Grand Final. Monday is the Busan day trip — a slow Daejeon morning, then KTX south to Busan for the afternoon, and a Busan → Seoul train in the evening (full plan in the Monday day entry). Book the Daejeon → Busan (≈10:30) and Busan → Seoul (≈18:30) legs at letskorail.com. Too wiped after MSI? Skip Busan and take a morning KTX Daejeon → Seoul instead.

While you're there, Sungsimdang (107 Expo-ro, near DCC II ⚠ verify hours) is Daejeon's legendary bakery — fried soboro and chive breads; queue moves fast. EXPO Science Park is adjacent to DCC II with open plazas, PokéStops, and gyms — useful for GO Fest raiding windows between matches. It's the former 1993 World's Fair site: a large open-air campus with wide walkways, gardens, and permanent science pavilions spread across roughly 1 km. Free to walk through; no entry fee for the public outdoor areas. Expect sun with little shade cover — hat and water matter here.

sections.title: Hongdae after dark
sections.body:
The university district around Hongik is Seoul's youth-and-nightlife core — street buskers, arcade halls, themed cafés, late-night eats, and anime goods. It's the natural evening base for nights you're not at the arena. Completely foreigner-accessible: no Korean contacts needed, no documented door discrimination (unlike parts of Gangnam), and most restaurants stay open until 04:00.

Two bars worth knowing — both walkable from Hongik Univ. Station:

  • Mike's Cabin — 94-13 Eoulmadang-ro, Mapo-gu (~5-min walk from Exit 8). Open Mon–Thu & Sun 18:00–02:00, Fri–Sat 18:00–05:00 (+82-2-326-1921). The go-to foreigner bar in Hongdae: reggaeton, pop, hip-hop, mixed local and international crowd. Cover ≈₩10,000 on weekends (≈ — confirm at door). Arrive before 22:30 to beat the queue.
  • Dokkaebi Party House — 22 Wausan-ro 17-gil, Seogyo-dong, Mapo-gu (~7-min walk from Hongik Univ. Station). One of Hongdae's biggest foreigner-friendly clubs: EDM to hip-hop, large mixed crowd. Fast-track entry + 1 free drink available via Klook in advance (≈ — verify cover on Klook before the trip).

Clubs fill at midnight and peak 01:00–03:00. Tuesday Jul 14 is the best night of this trip to stay late — no early alarm on Wednesday, and the flight home isn't until 21:30.

sections.title: PC bangs, arcades & LoL Park
sections.body: Korea's PC bangs — high-spec gaming cafés charging by the hour, often with food service — are everywhere and cheap; they're a rite of passage. Arcades lean into rhythm and crane games. League fans should check the LCK schedule: LoL Park in Jongno is the league's home studio/arena, and the Summer split resumes after MSI, so a live LCK match in Seoul may be on while you're there.

sections.title: Norebang, Han River & the ones Americans miss
sections.body:
Norebang (노래방) — private karaoke
Probably the most fun 2–3 hours in Seoul for a group. You rent a private room by the hour — typically ≈₩15,000–25,000/hour for a group room (≈, verify at the venue) — stock it with drinks from the lobby vending machine, and sing on a proper AV system with a catalogue of thousands including English titles. Completely private: no audience, no judgment, no one else watching. Hongdae is dense with noraebangs; look for neon signs or search Naver Map for 노래방. Walk-in is usually fine; late Friday and Saturday nights fill up, so arrive before midnight. Best fit for this trip: Tuesday evening (Jul 14) after the Secret Garden, since Wednesday's flight is in the evening and there's no early alarm.

Han River parks
Summer evenings, locals grab fried chicken from a nearby spot and eat on the grass by the river. Yeouido Hangang Park (Line 5, Yeouido Station, Exit 2–3) and Banpo Hangang Park (taxi ≈₩8,000–12,000 from Hongdae, or Line 2 to Dangsan then Line 9 to National Assembly (~20 min)) are the main ones. Bike rental available at both. The Banpo Bridge Rainbow Fountain runs free light-and-water shows daily at set times in summer (typically several showtimes between 19:00 and 22:00 — verify the current schedule on Naver Map or at the bridge before going). No entry fee, no language needed. A natural warm-evening option any night you're back in Hongdae with energy to spare — it's a short hop from Yeouido. Note Monday Jul 13 you'll be returning late from the Busan day trip (~22:00), so it fits better earlier in the week.

Korean corn dog (gamja hot dog)
A potato-battered hot dog — often with mozzarella inside, sometimes rolled in fries, dipped in your choice of sauces. Every street food zone in Myeongdong and Hongdae has them. ≈₩2,500–4,500 per piece (≈, check the stall). Messy and worth it.

Makgeolli (막걸리)
Creamy, lightly fizzy unfiltered rice wine; lower alcohol than soju and considerably less harsh. At a sit-down spot a ceramic jug is typically ≈₩8,000–15,000 (≈, verify). Pairs specifically with pajeon (scallion pancake) — Koreans associate the combination with rainy monsoon evenings, and after one July shower in Seoul you'll feel why immediately. Insadong has traditional makgeolli spots built into its alleys; Yeonnam-dong (walkable from Hongik Univ. Station Exit 3, ~5 min) has a more modern bar take.

Daiso
There are branches across Seoul including near Hongdae (search 'Daiso 홍대' on Naver Map). The Korean equivalent of a dollar store but actually good: travel accessories, skincare gadgets, character goods, snacks, phone cases — most things ₩1,000–5,000. Worth 30 minutes for cheap souvenirs and any trip supplies you realise you need. Completely self-service; no language required.

sections.title: Anime & game shopping
sections.body:
Animate Seoul (5F, AK& Mall, 188 Yanghwa-ro, Mapo-gu — Hongdae Station Exit 9; open ~11:00–22:00) is the largest anime specialist in the Hongdae area: figures, manga, Blu-ray, merchandise, and rotating pop-ups for seasonal titles. Yongsan i'Park Mall electronics floors carry games, trading cards, and figures — verify the current tenant mix at the mall, as renovations have shifted some vendors. KWANGYA@Seoul (SM's official store — B1, D-Tower, Seongsu; Ttukseom Station Exit 5, Line 2; ~10:30–20:00) has SM artists' albums, photocards, and a media zone. (The old SMTOWN COEX Artium closed in 2020.) Myeongdong K-pop street around Myeongdong Station has comprehensive stock across all agencies; go early or skip if the weekend crowds are too much.

HYBE — the Yongsan HYBE Insight museum has closed; HYBE now runs artist merch as rotating pop-ups (often AK Plaza Hongdae or near the Yongsan HQ). Check the Weverse Shop and HYBE socials the week before for what's live. LoL Park (3F Gran Seoul Building, 33 Jong-ro, Jongno-gu; open 11:00–20:00; Jonggak Station Exit 1) is the LCK's home venue with a free-to-browse exhibition floor, Riot merch store, and PC café. LCK Summer split resumes after MSI — check the schedule for a live match while you're there.

sections.title: What the dishes look like — and how to order

items.name: Samgyeopsal · 삼겹살
items.body: Thick pork-belly strips you grill at the table, wrapped in lettuce with garlic, ssamjang and kimchi. Order by the portion (인분, in-bun) — say "삼겹살 2인분" for two.

img.alt: Pork belly grilling on a tabletop grill with onion

items.name: Bibimbap · 비빔밥
items.body: Rice topped with seasoned vegetables, egg and red chili paste — mix it all together before eating. Point and say "비빔밥 하나" (one bibimbap). Dolsot (돌솥) means the sizzling stone bowl.

img.alt: Bibimbap with arranged vegetables and gochujang over rice

items.name: Tteokbokki · 떡볶이
items.body: Cylindrical rice cakes simmered in a sweet-spicy gochujang sauce — the classic street snack. Grab it from any pojangmacha (street stall) or a bunsik shop.

img.alt: Tteokbokki rice cakes in red sauce with sesame seeds

items.name: Chimaek · 치맥
items.body: Korean fried chicken with beer — the national evening ritual. "반반" (banban) gets you half plain-crispy, half sweet-spicy yangnyeom, the safe first order.

img.alt: Half crispy, half seasoned Korean fried chicken with pickled radish

items.name: Naengmyeon · 냉면
items.body: Cold buckwheat noodles in an icy broth (mul, 물) or with spicy sauce (bibim, 비빔) — exactly what July heat calls for. Snip them with the table scissors before eating.

img.file: KOCIS Mul-naengmyeon, Chilled Buckwheat Noodle Soup (4594756202).jpg
img.alt: Cold naengmyeon noodles in a metal bowl with ice, egg and beef

items.name: Gamjatang · 감자탕
items.body: A rich, spicy pork-spine-and-potato stew that bubbles at the table — hearty late-night or hangover food. Ordered by pot size for the group (소/중/대 = small/medium/large).

img.alt: Gamjatang pork spine stew with potato in red broth

items.name: Hotteok · 호떡
items.body: A chewy pancake stuffed with molten brown sugar, honey and cinnamon, pressed on a street griddle — winter-market perfect, sold year-round. Just point and hand over ₩1,000–2,000.

img.alt: Hotteok sweet pancakes cooking on a street griddle

items.name: Bingsu · 빙수
items.body: Fluffy shaved milk-ice piled with sweet red bean (pat, 팥), fruit and condensed milk — the café heat-beater. Patbingsu (팥빙수) is the classic; share one, they're big.

img.alt: Bowls of Korean bingsu shaved ice with red bean and fruit

sections.title: What to eat
sections.body:
Hit the obvious greats: Korean BBQ, chimaek (fried chicken and beer), tteokbokki, and market street food at Gwangjang — bindaetteok (mung-bean pancake), mayak gimbap, and live octopus if you're brave.

You're based in Jongno — old-Seoul heart, with Insadong, Ikseon-dong and Gwangjang Market a short walk away plus walk-in BBQ and café culture. Central for eating well without tourist-strip prices. For markets: Gwangjang Market (~5 min, Line 1 east 2 stops) and Namdaemun (Line 1 → Seoul Station, ~10 min). Myeongdong (K-beauty, street stalls) is a ~12-min walk — worth a half-day. Yongsan for beef and electronics (Line 1 south, ~10 min). In July heat, naengmyeon (cold buckwheat noodles) and bingsu (shaved-ice dessert) are exactly what you want. Café culture is everywhere when you need air-conditioning.

sections.title: Where locals review restaurants — apps and platforms
sections.body:
Google Maps ratings are thin in Korea — locals use different platforms entirely. These are what Koreans actually open before eating out:

  • Naver Map (네이버 지도) — the dominant platform. Search a restaurant, tap the listing and you'll see star ratings, visitor photos, and linked blog reviews (블로그 후기). The blog posts are often detailed and photo-heavy — even without Korean, the photos show whether the food looks right and whether the space is clean. In English mode, star ratings and photos are readable; use Papago for any text. This is the first check for anything in central Seoul or Hongdae. Download the app before the trip.
  • MangoPlate (망고플레이트) — Korea's dedicated restaurant discovery app, 200,000+ listings, English interface. Reviews are written by locals, not tourists — more candid than Western platforms. Especially good for Seoul neighbourhood spots, bakeries, and cafés. iOS and Android apps have English toggles.
  • Kakao Map (카카오맵) — integrated with the Kakao T taxi app, so a restaurant search can go directly to navigation or hailing a cab. Ratings are Korean-first but photos are universal.
  • Daytripkorea — English-friendly café and eatery finder with editorial curation; good for vetted shortlists rather than raw listings.

The practical flow: Search the place name on Naver Map first — below 4.0 on 1,000+ reviews, skip it; good photos and recent blog posts, trust it. Cross-check on MangoPlate if you're unsure. For new cafés and trending spots, MangoPlate surfaces them faster than Naver.

sections.title: Ordering in Korean restaurants — the mechanics
sections.body:
More accessible than it looks from the street. The mechanics that make it easy:

  • Kiosks — most chain restaurants and many AYCE BBQ spots have self-ordering touchscreens. Tap the flag icon for English, then browse photo menus and add items. No conversation. At AYCE BBQ kiosks also handle your meat refill orders during the meal.
  • Picture menus — most mid-range sit-down restaurants have laminated picture menus or a QR code that opens photos. Point at what you want, hold up fingers for quantity.
  • Papago camera — for text-only menus, open Papago, tap the camera icon, and point at the page. It overlays live Korean-to-English translation without you typing anything.
  • Walk in and sit — if a table is open, take it. A server will come. Most casual Korean restaurants don't seat you formally. Hold up fingers to show group size if asked.
  • Banchan is automatic and free — you'll receive a set of small side dishes (kimchi, egg rolls, salads) without ordering them. Always free, always refillable, always included. Don't order them or pay for them separately.
  • Call button — most Korean restaurants have a button on the table to summon a server. Press it to order more or ask for the bill. If there isn't one, yeogi-yo! (여기요) gets attention — completely normal, not rude.
  • Paying — walk up to the register when you're done. Bills are rarely brought to the table. Card is accepted almost everywhere; some market stalls are cash-only.
  • The phrase that handles most orders: point at the menu and say igeo juseyo (이거 주세요 — this one, please).

What to order beyond BBQ — the overlooked ones:

  • Jjigae (stew) — doenjang jjigae (fermented soybean), kimchi jjigae, or sundubu jjigae (silken tofu). Served boiling in a stone pot, ≈₩8,000–12,000 (≈), usually comes with rice and banchan. Available everywhere; good breakfast or recovery food after a long day.
  • Dolsot bibimbap — rice and vegetables in a hot stone bowl with a raw egg that cooks on contact; crispy rice forms at the bottom. Mix everything together before eating. Safe, filling, ≈₩12,000–15,000 (≈).
  • Somaek — the default way to drink in Korea: pour a shot of soju into a glass of beer and drink together. Every table at every chicken or pork spot does it. No special ordering needed — just ask for soju and beer.

sections.title: How to book restaurants in Korea
sections.body:
Korea's restaurant booking landscape is divided: popular spots require reservations; most neighbourhood BBQ joints and street food stalls are walk-in only. Here's how to book, in order of convenience for foreign visitors.

Catch Table Global — the primary tool for foreigners
Korea's dominant reservation platform now has an international version at catchtable.net (search 'Catchtable' on the App Store). No Korean phone number required, accepts Visa/Mastercard/Amex, available in English, 8,000+ restaurants listed. Deposits are standard (typically ₩10,000–30,000 per person, deducted from the bill or refunded after your visit). In-demand KBBQ restaurants, omakase spots, and Michelin-listed venues use Catch Table almost exclusively. Download before you leave.

Naver Map reservations — directly from the map
When you search a restaurant on Naver Map and see a blue 예약 (reservation) button on the listing, you can book directly in the app. As of June 2026, Naver now accepts passport-based identity verification for foreign visitors — no Korean phone number needed. Most popular mid-range spots use it; confirmation can be instant or take up to a day.

Phone — for smaller neighbourhood places
Some local restaurants take calls only. Most won't answer in English — ask your Airbnb host to call, or message through the restaurant's Naver/Kakao Map listing (many respond faster to a chat message than a call).

Walk-in reality check for this trip: Neighbourhood BBQ joints, ramen counters, and market stalls are walk-in only. For the specific restaurants in this guide: Ungteori Meats now recommends booking via Catch Table — weekday lunch is the most reliable walk-in window; weekend evenings queue. MUSSO and Chicken Baengi are reliably walk-in. Gobang Sikdang in Daejeon should be called on weekends (042-863-2104). If you're set on a specific spot for a meaningful night, book it before you land.

sections.title: Where to eat — picks near your route
sections.body:
Two clusters: near-base picks in Jongno for everyday meals, and Hongdae picks (near Hongik University Station, Line 2) for your nights out there. All prices ≈ — verify before you go.

Near the Jongno base: walk-in Korean BBQ, kalguksu and bunsik line Jongno and Insadong 5–10 min away, and the Gwangjang Market food alleys (below) are two Line-1 stops east. For the arrival-night dinner, any Jongno samgyeopsal joint does the job.

Hongdae street food — the pedestrian zone and Wausan-ro strip. Any evening you're over there. No booking. Tteokbokki, hotteok, skewers, grilled corn — ≈₩2,000–5,000 per item.

AYCE Korean BBQ — budget pork belly (arrival-night pick):

  • Ungteori Meats (엉터리생고기) — 118 Eoulmadang-ro, Mapo-gu. From Hongik Univ. Station: ~10 min walk. Open 11:00–23:30 daily. ≈₩14,800 pp; confirm on Creatrip. Fresh (not frozen) samgyeopsal. A Hongdae-night pick (from the Jongno base, save it for the Jul 10 raid evening). Reserve on Catch Table Global for weekend evenings — weekday lunch is the most reliable walk-in window. Naver Map.

AYCE Korean BBQ — mid-range / beef option:

  • MUSSO Hongdae (무쏘 홍대입구역점) — 131 Eoulmadang-ro (지층/basement), Mapo-gu; Hongik Univ. Station Exit 8, ~2-min walk. Open ≈12:00–01:00 daily — not 24 hr (⚠ confirm same-day for a late visit). ≈₩20,800–23,800 pp (beef; shrimp and yukhoe at the higher tier). Walk-in. Good for a Jul 14 Hongdae send-off dinner or a late bite after MSI. Naver Map.

KBBQ backups — if Ungteori or MUSSO are full:

  • Palsaik Samgyupsal (팔색삼겹살) — the 8-flavour pork belly concept: same cut marinated in eight sauces (garlic, herb, ginseng, curry, mushroom, red wine, soybean paste, original) served together. Multiple Hongdae branches; bookable on Klook if you want to pre-pay. Walk-in usually possible early evening weekdays. ≈₩13,000–16,000 pp ⚠ — confirm at venue. Naver Map.
  • Saemaul Sikdang (새마을식당 홍대점) — 144 Eoulmadang-ro, Mapo-gu. Popular chain known for staff-assisted grilling; the server manages the fire, so it's the most hands-off KBBQ option. Server brings water kimchi broth as a palate cleanser between cuts. ≈₩13,000–17,000 pp ⚠. Walk-in. Naver Map.
  • GIT TTEUL (깃들) — small neighbourhood BBQ on Jandari-ro 3an-gil (the indie bar and café alley running parallel to the main pedestrian zone). Authentic, locally-frequented, moderate prices, less tourist-facing than the main strip. Walk-in. ≈₩15,000–20,000 pp ⚠. Naver Map.

Chimaek (fried chicken + beer):

  • Chicken Baengi (치킨뱅이 홍대점) — 127 Eoulmadang-ro, Mapo-gu. From Hongik Univ. Station: ~10 min walk. Mon–Thu 16:00–01:00; Fri–Sun 14:00–01:00 ⚠. ≈₩10,000–20,000 pp; confirm at venue. Garlic chicken is the standout. Walk-in; busy Fri/Sat after 20:00 — arrive early. Best on Day 5 (Sun Jul 12) if the Daejeon pair returns early. Naver Map.

Cold noodles (July heat):

  • Yookssam Naengmyeon (육쌈냉면 홍대2호점) — 35 Wausan-ro 23-gil (2F), Mapo-gu; near Hongik Univ. Station Exit 9, ~5-min walk. Open ≈11:00–22:30 (⚠ one listing shows 10:30–21:00; confirm on yookssam.com). Naengmyeon set with charcoal-grilled bulgogi; ≈₩15,000–18,000 pp ⚠. Walk-in for lunch; dinner can queue. Naver Map.

Gwangjang Market (광장시장) — 88 Changgyeonggung-ro, Jongno-gu. From the Jongno base: Line 1 east 2 stops to Jongno 5-ga, Exit 7–8 (~5 min) — the market's on your doorstep now. Food vendors from 09:00; eating alleys open most days — ⚠ individual stall hours vary. Cash only at most stalls. Bindaetteok ≈₩4,000, mayak gimbap ≈₩3,000. A weekday is emptier; it fits as the Monday fallback if you skip Busan (see the Monday contingencies), or as a lunch stop near Insadong on Tue Jul 14 after the Secret Garden. Walk-in only.

Bingsu:

  • Sulbing (설빙) — find the nearest Hongdae branch; confirm on sulbing.com. Open ~12:00–24:00. Injeolmi bingsu ≈₩8,900; mango/melon ≈₩14,900–15,900 ⚠. No booking. Perfect mid-afternoon on any hot day.

Daejeon — near DCC II (Expo district, Yuseong-gu):

  • Sungsimdang DCC Branch (성심당 DCC점) — 107 Expo-ro, Yuseong-gu, same street as DCC II, short walk. Open daily 08:00–22:00. Legendary Daejeon bakery; fried soboro and chive breads. Walk-in; grab breakfast before the arena on Sat Jul 11. Naver Map.
  • Ossi Kalguksu Doryong (오씨칼국수 도룡점) — Doryong-dong 413, Yuseong-gu, ~0.2 mi from DCC II. Highly-rated knife-cut noodle soup (the Doryong branch and the Samseong-dong original are the genuine ones — ignore copycats). Open ≈11:00–21:00 — ⚠ weekend hours unconfirmed; call ahead (042-861-9972). ≈₩9,000–12,000 pp. Walk-in. Naver Map.
  • Cham-i-mat Gamjatang (참이맛감자탕&순대국 대전엑스포점) — 엑스포로151번길 19 (same building as Gobang Sikdang, different floor), ~5 min walk from I-Hotel. Open 11:00–22:00 daily (last order 21:30; Sun split: 11:00–15:00 & 17:00–22:00). Pork-bone stew and blood-sausage soup — hearty recovery food. ≈₩9,000–13,000 pp. Walk-in. Naver Map.
  • Gobang Sikdang Expo Branch (고반식당 대전엑스포점) — 엑스포로151번길 19 (2F), ~5–10 min walk from I-Hotel. Mon–Fri 16:00–24:00; Sat–Sun 12:00–23:00. Premium aged Korean pork grilled by staff. ≈₩20,000–30,000 pp ⚠. Book ahead weekends: 042-863-2104. Naver Map. Best: Sun Jul 12 post-match celebration.

Post-match dinner in Daejeon — pick by how late it runs. The Grand Final (Sun, 15:00 start) likely ends 18:00–20:30; the Lower Bracket Final (Sat, 17:00 start) can run to ~22:00.

  • Wrapped by ~21:00: any pick above — Gobang Sikdang (book ahead for the celebration grill), Cham-i-mat (gamjatang, last order 21:30), or Ossi Kalguksu if you arrive before 21:00.
  • 21:00–23:00: Gobang Sikdang is the safest sit-down (kitchen to 24:00 Mon–Fri / 23:00 Sat–Sun); Cham-i-mat only if you make the 21:30 last order.
  • After ~23:00, or for a night out: Dunsan-dong (Seo-gu, ~15–20 min taxi) is Daejeon's nightlife strip — 24-hour budae-jjigae, chimaek and makgeolli bars (search '둔산동' on Naver Map for what's open now). For chimaek closer to the hotel, the BBQ / Kyochon / BHC chains have Yuseong branches open to ≈01:00 — check Naver Map day-of. Always-open fallback: the GS25 / CU and pojangmacha tents around the I-Hotel.

Jul 10 lunch — near Gyeongbokgung (Gwanghwamun area):

  • Gwanghwamun Mijin (광화문미진) — 종로구 종로 19 (르메이에르종로타운 1F). From Gyeongbokgung main gate: ~10 min walk south toward Gwanghwamun Square. Open 10:30–21:00 daily (last order 20:30). 2026 Michelin Bib Gourmand. Cold buckwheat noodles (냉메밀) and warm versions; ≈₩12,000/bowl. Walk-in; lunch queue 12:00–13:00 — arrive before noon or at 13:30. Best fit: right after Gyeongbokgung, before the Bukchon afternoon.

Jul 14 lunch — near Anguk / Changdeokgung:

  • Bukchon Sonmandu (북촌손만두) — 종로구 관훈동 42-2, near Insadong. From Changdeokgung gate: Anguk Station Exit 6, ~10-min walk south. Open 11:00–20:30 daily. Three-generation mandu specialist: fried dumplings (튀김만두), steamed dumplings, manduguk. ≈₩3,500/serving, cold noodles ≈₩5,500. Walk-in only. Best fit: after the 10:30 Huwon tour, before Insadong shopping.

Convenience store — the underrated late-night move:

  • GS25 and CU everywhere, open 24 h: triangle gimbap ~₩1,500, instant ramen ~₩1,500, egg salad sandwich ~₩2,500, tteokbokki cups. After a full GO Fest / MSI day, often the right call.

sections.title: Breakfast near the base
sections.body:
Korea doesn't do big hotel-style breakfasts, but Jongno has good options from 07:00–07:30:

  • Isaac Toast — the toasted-egg-sandwich chain has branches around Jongno/Insadong; look for the yellow signs. Opens ~07:00. Korean-style toasted egg sandwiches ~₩3,500–5,500 (≈); 5–10 min queue on weekdays, longer on weekends. Quick, cheap, no language needed — point at the menu pictures. The best fast breakfast before an early palace day.
  • Paris Baguette / Tous les Jours — both chains have branches within a few minutes of the base. Opens ~08:00. Coffee, croissants, egg sandwiches, sweet pastries — reliable, air-conditioned, and fast. Good on days when you're heading somewhere early.
  • Cafés in Ikseon-dong / Insadong — the hanok lanes a few minutes east are dense with independent cafés that open from 08:00 (some earlier on weekends). Most serve egg-toast, bagels, or light breakfast sets alongside coffee. A block off the main street is cheaper and often very good.
  • Convenience store (GS25 / CU) — everywhere, open 24 h. Triangle gimbap ~₩1,500, egg-salad sandwich ~₩2,500, hot coffee from machine ~₩600. The pragmatic call on early departure days, post-late-night mornings, or any day you want to eat at the base. GS25 and CU are within a 1-min walk anywhere in Jongno.

sections.title: Late-night food after midnight
sections.body:
Seoul's late-night food scene runs well past midnight. In Hongdae — where you'll be for the raid and send-off nights — it's right outside the door.

  • Hongdae restaurant strip — most restaurants in Hongdae run until 04:00, some 24 hours. After clubs: ramen shops, pojangmacha tents (street-food stalls), and simya sikdang (late-night diners serving comfort Korean food from 22:00 to sunrise). Mapo-gu's designated pojangmacha zone near Hongdae concentrates the best street tents legally. No transit, no planning — just walk out and find it.
  • MUSSO Hongdae — open until ≈01:00 (not 24 hr; listed in the picks section above); works as a late AYCE option up to last call. ~2-min walk from Hongik Univ. Station Exit 8. After ~01:00, the convenience stores and pojangmacha tents take over.
  • Boseung Hoegwan (보승회관 명동점) — 13 Myeongdong 9-gil, Jung-gu, Seoul. Open 24 hours. The standby for post-midnight hangover soup (haejang-guk): pork rib soup, ramen, and white sliced pork. ≈₩10,000–20,000 per person (≈, verify on-site). 5-min walk from Euljiro 1-ga Station Exit 6. Worth the ~30-min trip from Hongdae if you specifically want haejang-guk; otherwise the Hongdae strip has you covered.
  • Convenience store (GS25 / CU) — always the reliable backup: instant ramen at a hot-water station, tteokbokki cups, kimbap, snacks. No closing time.

sections.title: Shopping game plan — low-hassle route
sections.body:
The idea: most of the load is gifts for other people (K-beauty, K-pop). So buy the heavy for-others stuff last and nearest the airport, keep everything else central and hands-free, and let tax refunds + bag storage do the carrying. Three rules:

  1. Bulk K-beauty on departure morning — Olive Young Jonggak, across from the base → straight into checked bags or the airport luggage courier → VAT refund at ICN. You never carry a skincare haul through the July heat.
  2. Use in-store tax refund + bag storage. Olive Young Myeongdong Town knocks the ~10% VAT off at the register (passport out) and holds your bags so you shop hands-free.
  3. Stay central and covered. The Jongno–Insadong–Myeongdong core is walkable and underground-connected — good in a downpour or mid-30s heat. Only Stüssy needs a river crossing, folded into Tuesday.

By day:

  • Fri Jul 10 — esports, no detour. LoL Park Riot store is at your base (Jonggak Exit 1); T1 Base Camp shop (Hongdae, 10:30–22:00) folds into that evening's raid night. Both merch stops happen where you already are.
  • Tue Jul 14 — the main run, one clean sweep. After the 10:30 Secret Garden: central gifts first — Insadong souvenirs, then Myeongdong (Olive Young flagship K-beauty with VAT-at-register + bag storage, Line Friends, K-pop street). Then Line 3 to Apgujeong for Stüssy + Gentle Monster, and finish right there with dinner at Samwon Garden (garden galbi) — one river crossing, no backtrack. (Prefer to end central? Loop back to Insadong for A Flower Blossom on the Rice.)
  • Wed Jul 15 — the bulk haul, then go. Slow morning at Olive Young Jonggak for the big gift load → courier / checked bags → 6002 → VAT at ICN.

K-pop stores — corrected: HYBE Insight (the Yongsan museum) has closed — HYBE merch is pop-ups now, check the Weverse Shop the week before. SMTOWN COEX Artium closed in 2020; SM's store is now KWANGYA@Seoul in Seongsu (Ttukseom, Line 2; ~10:30–20:00) — a separate trip, or skip it for the central Myeongdong K-pop options.

Store quick-reference (⚠ confirm hours before you go):

  • Olive Young Myeongdong Town — ~10:00–22:30; 2 floors, in-store VAT refund, bag storage.
  • Olive Young Jonggak — across from the base; the departure-morning haul.
  • Stüssy Seoul Chapter — 42 Apgujeong-ro 46-gil, Gangnam; 11:00–20:00; Kasina cafe upstairs.
  • LoL Park Riot store — Gran Seoul, Jonggak Exit 1; ~10:00–19:00.
  • T1 Base Camp — 147 Yanghwa-ro, Hongdae (B1); shop 10:30–22:00.
  • Line Friends Square — Myeongdong; ~11:00–22:00.
  • KWANGYA@Seoul (SM) — Seongsu, Ttukseom Exit 5; ~10:30–20:00.

VAT refund: spend ≥₩15,000 at a tax-free shop and show your passport for the instant discount, or claim the stamped receipts at the ICN departures kiosk before check-in.

sections.title: Shopping in Seoul
sections.body:
You're based in Jongno — Insadong crafts, Gwangjang Market and LoL Park on your doorstep. By district from Jonggak (Line 1): Insadong — 5–10 min walk (crafts, souvenirs); Myeongdong — ~12 min walk or Line 1 to Euljiro for K-beauty (Olive Young flagship, Innisfree); Dongdaemun — Line 1 east ~8 min (late-night shopping to 04:00); Gangnam / COEX — ~30 min (Line 1 to City Hall → Line 2) for malls (SM's KWANGYA store is in Seongsu — see the shopping plan). Tax-free shops offer a VAT refund on purchases over ₩15,000 — instant in-store or at the airport kiosk on departure.

Streetwear in Hongdae (a Line-2 hop from central Seoul): Musinsa Standard flagship (144 Yanghwa-ro, Mapo-gu; open 11:00–21:00; near Exit 9) — Korean casualwear at a wide price range. The alley cluster around Wausan-ro (Mahagrid, SOUR, indie labels) rewards a slow browse — 10–15 min walk from Hongik Univ. Station. Gentle Monster Hongdae (54 Dokmak-ro 7-gil; open 12:00–22:00) is an art-installation eyewear store — free to walk through. Ader Error has moved to Seongsu (Seongdong-gu, Line 2 ~30 min); worth the trip if design-forward Korean fashion is your thing.

Esports, anime & gaming: LoL Park (now CHZZK LoL Park) — the official LCK arena at Gran Seoul, 33 Jong-ro, Jongno-gu; Jonggak Station (Line 1) Exit 1. The Riot Store, café and exhibits are open to walk-ins ≈10:00–19:00 (arena seating needs an LCK match ticket); the in-house PC bang runs 24 h. Visit Seoul listing. Animate Hongdae — Japan's anime-goods chain on 5F of AK Plaza (188 Yanghwa-ro), right at Hongik Univ. Station Exit 4; open 11:00–21:40; figures, manga, game and VTuber merch. Yongsan i'Park Mall (Yongsan Station, ~10 min via Line 1 south) stacks electronics, trading cards and figures across several floors.

sections.title: Souvenirs — Korea
items: K-beauty from Olive Young (sheet masks, skincare)
Korean snacks (honey-butter chips, Pepero, Market O)
Kakao Friends / Line Friends character goods
A celadon or craft piece from Insadong

sections.title: Collectibles & chain merch
sections.body: For collectors: a Starbucks 'Been There' Seoul mug, and the Kakao Friends and Line Friends flagship stores (Hongdae and Gangnam) for character-brand exclusives you won't find at home.

sections.title: Quick reference — when you're unsure what to do
sections.body:
Open this when no one can agree on a plan.

  • Hungry, near the base: any Jongno/Insadong BBQ walk-in, or the Gwangjang Market alleys (2 stops east). In Hongdae for the night? Yookssam Naengmyeon or Chicken Baengi.
  • Want KBBQ tonight: Walk the side streets off Wausan-ro after 17:00 — a dozen AYCE spots, no reservations needed mid-week. Budget ≈₩15,000–25,000 pp.
  • Fastest possible meal: GS25 or CU — triangle gimbap (≈₩1,500), corn dog, instant ramen at the counter. Genuinely good and a Seoul experience.
  • Hot and need AC: any café, the Jonggak/Jongno underground arcades, or COEX Mall (Samseong, Line 2). Free to enter, browse as long as you want.
  • Nothing to do in the afternoon: Han River — Line 5 to Yeouido Station, buy fried chicken from the strip at Exit 3–4, sit on the grass by the water. Free, always worth it.
  • Want to raid but don't want to sightsee: Jongno, Insadong and Cheonggyecheon are gym-dense near the base; Hongdae and Sinchon for a bigger cluster. Drop a Lure at a café table and grind.
  • Evening with no plan: the Ikseon-dong and Insadong lanes on your doorstep, or ride to Hongdae's pedestrian zone — buskers, street food, energy toward midnight.
  • After midnight: MUSSO (AYCE Korean BBQ, open until ≈01:00) or any GS25 with ramen — after ~01:00 it's the convenience stores and pojangmacha tents. Mike's Cabin if someone wants a bar.
  • Daejeon: gap between events: Hanbat Arboretum (free, walkable from EXPO, great PokéStop density) or the EXPO Science Park grounds. Both have shade.

sections.title: 2026 Tax Refund Optimization
checklist: Passport out at every checkout — in-store immediate refund requires it (no passport = no same-day refund)
₩15,000 minimum per receipt to qualify; split a large purchase if needed to get separate qualifying receipts
Olive Young: split purchases across 3–4 transactions in ₩100,000 increments — each qualifies independently for the refund counter
Lotte Mart / large supermarkets (e.g. the Seoul Station branch): free boxes + tape at the exit — staff will pack snacks and groceries for the flight at no cost
ICN Terminal 1: tax-refund kiosk in the departures level before security — bring all stamped receipts; refund issued as cash or card credit on the spot

sections.title: Road of Legends 2026 — what to know

cards.label: Event window
cards.body: Mon Jul 6 – Fri Jul 10 (ends 23:59 KST). You land Thursday Jul 9 and catch the final two days.

cards.label: Raid passes
cards.body: Up to 9 free Raid Passes/day from Gym photo discs.

cards.label: Collectible
cards.body: Pokémon caught from any raid Jul 6–12 may earn a Special Pokémon Background collectible.

sections.title: Raid Hour rotation — Thu & Fri
sections.note: Friday evening is one of the best raiding windows of the trip — full day plan in the day-by-day entry.

windows.name: Crowned Forms
windows.tip: Weak to Fire and Ground only — the same team handles both. You just flew 14 hours: use 2–3 passes if you're feeling fresh, save most for tomorrow.

windows.name: Origin Duo
windows.tip: Best Master League forms of their species (Roar of Time / Spacial Rend). Save 4–5 passes here.

windows.name: Primal Duo
windows.tip: Groudon (Ground/Fire) has a quadruple Water weakness — your Kyogre counters make it trivial. Coordinate 5+ trainers for Primal tier. Groudon is also your best Xurkitree counter Sunday — a priority XL Candy target.

sections.title: Raid counter charts — Road of Legends Jul 9 & 10

bosses.name: Crowned Sword Zacian
bosses.note: Thursday Jul 9 all day + Raid Hour 18:00–19:00 KST. Steel typing neutralises all the usual Fairy counters (Ghost/Dark/Bug do nothing useful here). Bring the same Fire/Ground team for Zamazenta right after.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Fire and Ground only. Steel cancels out every type Fairy is normally weak to — no Ghost, Dark, or Bug. Fire hits harder overall (Reshiram, Mega Blaziken). Primal Groudon covers both weaknesses in one slot. This same team works identically for Crowned Shield Zamazenta.

bosses.name: Crowned Shield Zamazenta
bosses.note: Thursday Jul 9 all day + Raid Hour 18:00–19:00 KST. Fighting/Steel is an unusual type combination — Psychic and Fairy (usually great vs Fighting) are fully neutralised by the Steel typing. Identical weakness profile to Crowned Sword Zacian: Fire and Ground only.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Fire and Ground only. Steel neutralises the Fighting weakness to Psychic, Flying, and Fairy. Reuse the exact same team you built for Crowned Zacian — there's no need to swap anything.

bosses.name: Origin Forme Dialga
bosses.note: Friday Jul 10 all day + Raid Hour 18:00–19:00 KST. The best Dialga form for Master League — knows Roar of Time. High priority for XL Candy. Both Fighting and Ground work; Ground slightly favoured due to Primal Groudon coverage.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Fighting and Ground. Steel's Dragon resistance means Dragon-type attackers do nothing here. Primal Groudon (from earlier in the same Raid Hour) is the best single counter if you have it; Mega Garchomp is the accessible Mega lead. Terrakion and Keldeo lead the Fighting options.

bosses.name: Origin Forme Palkia
bosses.note: Friday Jul 10 all day + Raid Hour 18:00–19:00 KST. The best Palkia form for Master League — knows Spacial Rend. Dragon and Fairy both hit super-effectively. If you raided Rayquaza at GO Fest Day 1, bring it.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Dragon and Fairy. Water neutralises Dragon's usual Rock and Ice weaknesses. Dragon-type moves hit harder overall; Mega Salamence is the top Mega lead. Fairy picks (Xerneas, Mega Gardevoir) are great if Dragon is short on trainers — Fairy moves are unresisted here.

bosses.name: Primal Kyogre
bosses.shiny_note: Same shiny model as regular Kyogre (black/dark highlights)
bosses.trainers: 5–10 trainers (Primal tier has significantly more HP than 5-star)
bosses.note: Friday Jul 10 Primal Raid Hour 19:00–20:00 KST. Pure Water — Electric hits hardest. Kyogre is also the top counter for Primal Groudon in the same raid hour; stack Raid Passes between both Primals.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Grass and Electric. Electric moves deal slightly more damage overall. Zekrom (Fusion Bolt) is the single best counter; Shadow Raikou and Xurkitree are close behind. Primal tier — 5+ trainers recommended, more if under Level 35. Coordinate before the hour starts.

bosses.name: Primal Groudon
bosses.shiny_note: Same shiny model as regular Groudon (gold/yellow highlights)
bosses.note: Friday Jul 10 Primal Raid Hour 19:00–20:00 KST. Ground/Fire = quadruple Water weakness (the only type that hits both super-effectively). Your Kyogre literally counters this boss. Priority target: Primal Groudon is your best counter for Xurkitree on Sunday, and top-tier Ground attacker for Master League XL.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Water only — but it is a quadruple weakness (both Ground and Fire take 2.56× from Water in GO). Ice, Grass, and other types that usually hit Ground well are resisted by Fire. Bring nothing but Water. Kyogre (available in the same Raid Hour) is the single best attacker; Mega Swampert and Mega Gyarados are the top Mega options.

sections.title: Already did Copenhagen? Your Global divergence
sections.note: You raided GO Fest Copenhagen in person (Fælledparken, Jun 12–14) — much of the Global headline content repeats. Net effect: skip Zeraora and the Mewtwos unless you want the shiny; make Xurkitree your Sunday must; hoover up the legendaries and incense regionals Copenhagen never offered.

tiers.tier: ✅ Skip / deprioritize — already done at Copenhagen
tiers.body: Copenhagen's in-person Mewtwo already knows both Counter + Psystrike (better than either single-move Global raid) and gave you Primal Kyogre/Groudon (outclass the Global 5★ forms). Only worth a raid now for a missing shiny or extra Mega Energy/XL Candy. Shiny Wash Rotom was Copenhagen-only — not in Global at all.

tiers.tier: 🔼 Fresh at Global — your real priorities
tiers.body: Xurkitree (Sun Verdant 13–16, Daejeon) is the single most valuable target — an Asia-Pacific regional Ultra Beast Copenhagen never had. Everything else here is a legendary or collection mon Copenhagen's smaller 5★ pool (Moltres/Mewtwo/Entei only) simply didn't include.

sections.title: Habitat rotation — Sat & Sun
sections.note: Habitats cycle every 3 hours (10:00–19:00 KST); Mega + 5★ bosses rotate hourly within each window. ⚠ Niantic finalises exact boss lists days before — re-check LeekDuck the week of Jul 6.

windows.mega: Super Mega all day: Mega Mewtwo X (knows Counter) — every habitat.

windows.name: Earthforged Domain

sections.title: Priority targets — which raids to spend passes on
sections.note: Every GO Fest legendary 5★ and Super Mega is ✨ 1/20 shiny — the best odds in the game. Tuned to your weekend: Saturday and Sunday are both Daejeon all day for all three of you (two of you in the arena for the LBF/Grand Final, the third raiding EXPO). Already did Copenhagen? See the divergence card above — it reorders these for you. Pass math: Sat — 3–5 on Mega Mewtwo X, rest on Dragonflight. Sun — 3–4 on Mega Mewtwo Y, 2–3 on Xurkitree, rest on Twilight around the Grand Final.

tiers.tier: S — do these no matter what
tiers.body: Mega Mewtwo X (Sat, every habitat) — global debut, best Psychic attacker; weak to Flying/Fairy. Mega Mewtwo Y (Sun) — stronger Psychic; weak to Ghost/Dark/Bug. Xurkitree (Sun Verdant 13–16) — Korea-only regional, top non-shadow Electric attacker; raid before the 15:00 Grand Final. Weak to Ground.

tiers.tier: A — high value if passes/time allow
tiers.body: Sat Dragonflight (around the LBF): Rayquaza (best F2P Dragon + Candy XL), Groudon (top Ground + your Xurkitree counter), Kyogre (top Water), Reshiram/Zekrom (top Fire/Electric). Sun Earthforged (morning): Therian Landorus/Thundurus, Heatran, Origin Dialga/Palkia. Sun Twilight (around the GF): Latios, Darkrai, Zacian, Zamazenta.

Giratina Origin (Sat Astral, top ML Ghost) · Deoxys Attack (Sun Verdant, PvE) · Necrozma (Sun Verdant, fusion fodder) · Nihilego (Sun Verdant, PvP) · the Regi trio + Regigigas (Sun Earthforged) · Tapu quartet (Sun Verdant, PvP Fairy).

tiers.tier: Low priority — persists after the event
tiers.body: Runs in Shadow Raids all weekend, separate from the habitat rotation. Grab one if a gym is handy, but it's available through Aug 4 — not urgent.

sections.title: Raid counter charts — GO Fest Global 2026

bosses.name: Mega Mewtwo X
bosses.shiny_note: Global debut — first appearance in Pokémon GO
bosses.trainers: 10–20 trainers (Super Mega tier — harder than standard Mega)
bosses.note: Saturday Jul 11 only — appears in every habitat, so you can raid it in Seoul in the morning and again near the arena in Daejeon. Branching Research lets you choose the X or Y encounter.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Flying, Fairy, and Ghost. The Fighting secondary type does not remove Ghost weakness — Ghost still hits Psychic for 2×. Flying is the highest-DPS type: Mega Rayquaza (Dragon Ascent) is best if you have it; otherwise lead Mega Pidgeot (Saturday's rotation) or Mega Gardevoir for Fairy. Dawn Wings Necrozma is the top Ghost pick and fits both Mewtwo forms.

bosses.name: Mega Mewtwo Y
bosses.shiny_note: Global debut — first appearance in Pokémon GO
bosses.note: Sunday Jul 12 only — appears in every habitat, so raid it morning and afternoon around the Grand Final. Pure Psychic — more counters available than Mega X.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Bug, Ghost, Dark. Ghost and Dark are the strongest damage types here. Dawn Wings Necrozma is the single best counter in the game; Mega Tyranitar and Mega Gengar round out the premium trio. Shadow Tyranitar or Shadow Hydreigon are excellent budget shadow leads.

bosses.name: Rayquaza
bosses.note: Saturday Dragonflight Summit (4–7 PM) — live in Daejeon around the LBF; raid near DCC II / EXPO Science Park. Top F2P Dragon attacker and Candy XL for Master League. (Mega Rayquaza is not a raid boss this event — use Mega Salamence as your Dragon Mega lead.)
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Ice (2×), Dragon, Fairy, Rock. Ice hits for double super-effective — always lead Ice. Standard 5-star difficulty; 2–3 high-level trainers handle it comfortably.

bosses.name: Kyogre
bosses.note: Saturday Dragonflight Summit (4–7 PM) — live in Daejeon around the LBF. Top Water attacker and Candy XL; doubles as a Groudon and Xurkitree counter.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Grass, Electric. Zekrom and Shadow Raikou are the top Electric picks; Roserade or Kartana for Grass. Zekrom's Fusion Bolt is the hardest-hitting charged move here.

bosses.name: Groudon
bosses.note: Saturday Dragonflight Summit (4–7 PM) — live in Daejeon around the LBF. Top Ground attacker, Candy XL for Master League, and your best Xurkitree counter on Sunday.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Water, Grass, Ice. Water hits hardest — Kyogre (in the same habitat) and Mega Swampert / Mega Gyarados lead. Kartana and Mega Sceptile bring Grass; Mamoswine and Galarian Darmanitan bring Ice.

bosses.name: Giratina (Origin Forme)
bosses.note: Saturday Astral Tides habitat (1–4 PM). Top PvP pick for Master League; worth stacking Candy XL.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Ghost, Ice, Dragon, Dark, Fairy. Ghost and Dragon hit hardest. Shadow Gengar with Lick + Shadow Ball is the accessible shadow lead. Dawn Wings Necrozma is premier.

bosses.name: Xurkitree
bosses.shiny_note: Korea is in the Asia-Pacific region where Xurkitree appears — region-locked Ultra Beast, you can't get it at home
bosses.note: Sunday Verdant Anomaly (1–4 PM), Daejeon — the Asia-Pacific regional Ultra Beast and a top non-shadow Electric attacker. Raid it before the 3 PM Grand Final; it's the single most location-exclusive thing on the weekend.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Ground only (pure Electric) — and Ground is immune to its Electric attacks, so a full Ground team takes almost no damage. Lead Mega Garchomp or Excadrill; Groudon (from Saturday) and Therian Landorus are premium.

bosses.name: Necrozma
bosses.note: Sunday Verdant Anomaly (1–4 PM). Base Necrozma — fuse with Solgaleo for Dusk Mane or Lunala for Dawn Wings (the game's best Ghost attacker). Worth a couple of raids as fusion fodder.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Bug, Ghost, Dark (pure Psychic — same profile as Mega Mewtwo Y, so reuse that team). Mega Gengar, Dawn Wings Necrozma and Mega Tyranitar are premier; Chandelure and Shadow Tyranitar are accessible.

bosses.name: Darkrai
bosses.note: Sunday Twilight Battlefield (4–7 PM). Strong PvE attacker; worth multiple shiny hunts.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Fighting, Bug, Fairy. Fighting-type attackers hit hardest. Mega Lopunny is the best Mega boost here; Lucario and Conkeldurr are accessible shadow leads.

bosses.name: Zacian (Hero of Many Battles)
bosses.note: Sunday Twilight Battlefield (4–7 PM). Top Master League meta pick; high priority.
bosses.strategy: Weak to: Poison, Steel. Mega Beedrill and Nihilego are premier Poison picks; Metagross and Dialga cover Steel. Poison hits hardest — build your best Poison team.
